name: pastWeek
doc: DateSpan for last 7 days as 'today-7days..today'

name: toRadians
doc: Convert angle in degrees to an angle in radians.

name: haystackRead
doc:
Perform Haystack REST API call to read single entity with filter.
The filter is an expression like `readAll`.  Return result as dict.
If the record is not found, then return null or raise UnknownRecErr
based on checked flag.  Also see `haystack::Client.read`.

name: ioDir
doc:
Read a directory listing, return a grid with cols:
- 'uri': Uri for handle to read/write the file
- 'name': filename string
- 'mimeType': file mime type or null if unknown
- 'dir':  marker if file is a sub-directory or null
- 'size': size of file in bytes or null
- 'mod':  modified timestamp or null if unknown

If the I/O handle does not map to a file in the virtual file system then
throw an exception.

ioDir(`io/`)             // read files in project's io/ directory
ioDir(`fan://haystack`)  // read files in pod

name: ioReadZinc
doc: Read a [Zinc]`docHaystack::Zinc` file into memory as a Haystack data type.

name: dayOfYear
doc:
Given a DateTime or Date, return the day of the year.  The result
is a number between 1 and 365 (or 1 to 366 if a leap year).

name: instances
doc:
Lookup instances from Xeto libs as a list of dicts.

Scope may one of the following:
- null: all instances from all libs currently in the using scope
- lib: all instances declared in given library
- list of lib: all instances in given libraries

If the filter is null, then it filters the instances from the scope.

NOTE: this function returns the instances using Haystack level fidelity

Examples:
instances()                  // all instances in scope
specLib("icons").instances   // instances in a given library
instances(null, a and b)     // filter instances with filter expression

name: rstrip
doc: Axon function

name: uriPlusSlash
doc: Adding trailing slash to the URI.  See `sys::Uri.plusSlash`

name: haystackInvokeAction
doc:
Invoke a remote action on the given Haystack connector
and remote entity.  The id must be a Ref of the remote entity's
identifier and action is a Str action name.  If args are
specified, then they should be a Dict keyed by parameter
name.

name: ioHmac
doc:
Generate an HMAC message authentication as specified by RFC 2104.
See `sys::Buf.hmac`.

Example:
ioHmac("foo", "SHA-1", "secret").ioToBase64

name: hisSlice
doc:
Slice a source history grid by a single time window.  Slices
only include items from the source grid which are inclusive of
the window time range.  The starting and ending rows are always
the time boundaries of window with the values interpolated (or
null if they cannot be interpolated).

Example:

  // input data (dates ignored)
  ts, v0
  00:00, 20, true
  01:00, 30, false
  02:00, 40, null
  03:00, 50, true
  04:00, 60, false

  // slice function
  hisSlice(src, dateTime(date, 1:30), 2hr)

  // resulting grid
  ts, val
  1:30, 35, false
  2:00, 40, null
  3:00, 50, true
  3:30, 55, true

Note in the example above that we added a row at beginning and at the
end for the window boundaries.  The values of these boundary
rows are interpolated from the original data.

See [Windows and Slices]`ext-hisKit::doc#windowsAndSlices`

name: logDebug
doc: Log at the "debug" level - see `log`.

name: bitNot
doc: Bitwise not: '~a'

name: streamCol
doc:
Create a new stream for the cell values of the given column.
See `docHaxall::Streams#streamCol`.

name: readLink
doc:
Read a record Dict by its id for hyperlinking in a UI.  Unlike other
reads which return a Dict, this read returns the columns ordered in
the same order as reads which return a Grid.

name: parseTime
doc:
Parse a Str into a Time.  If the string cannot be parsed into a valid
Time and checked is false then return null, otherwise throw ParseErr.
See `sys::Time.toLocale` for pattern.

Examples:
parseTime("14:30", "h:mm")
parseTime("2:30pm", "k:mma")
parseTime("2:30:00pm", "k:mm:ssa")

name: projSync
doc: Block until all folio/project message queues are fully processed.

name: weatherSunriseTime
doc: Get sunrise for date and location.

name: projRebuild
doc:
Rebuild the current project in-place. This operation will shutdown the
project, then rebuild it by copying all recs and re-writing all history.
It supports the following options:
  - hisPageSize: (Duration) - the hisPageSize to use when re-writing the
  history. If not specified, the current hisPageSize is used.

Returns a Future that will be completed when the rebuild is finished. This
operation could take a long time for large databases.

name: choiceOf
doc:
Given a choice spec, return the most specific choice subtype
implemented by the instance.  If the instance implements zero or more
than one subtype of the choice, then return null or raise an exception
based on the checked flag.  The instance may be anything accepted
by the `toRec()` function.

Example:
choiceOf({discharge, duct}, DuctSection)  >>  DischargeDuct
choiceOf({hot, water}, Fluid)             >>  HotWater

name: toLocale
doc:
Get the localized string for the given tag name or qualified name.
If the key is formatted as "pod::name" then route to `sys::Env.locale`,
otherwise to `haystack::Etc.tagToLocale`.

name: ioReadJson
doc:
Read a JSON file into memory. This function can used to read any
arbitrary JSON nested object/array structure which can be accessed
as Axon dicts/lists.  The default decoding assumes Haystack 4 JSON
format (Hayson).  Also see `ioReadJsonGrid` if reading a Haystack
formatted grid.

Object keys which are not valid tag names will decode correctly
and can be used in-process.  But they will not serialize correctly
over the HTTP API.  You can use the 'safeNames' option to force object
keys to be safe tag names (but you will lose the original key names).

The following options are supported:
- v3: decode the JSON as Haystack 3
- v4: explicitly request Haystack 4 decoding (default)
- safeNames: convert object keys to safe tag names

name: specMetaOwn
doc:
Get the spec's own declared meta-data as dict.

NOTE: this function returns the meta using haystack level fidelity

Examples:
specMetaOwn(Date)  >>  {sealed, val:2000-01-01, doc:"...", pattern:"..."}

name: userReadById
doc:
Read a rec Dict from the user db by id.  If context user is not
su, then the user being read must be accessible to the current
context project.

name: isUpper
doc:
Is number an ASCII uppercase alphabetic char: A-Z

Examples:
isUpper("A".get(0))  >>  true
isUpper("a".get(0))  >>  false
isUpper("5".get(0))  >>  false

name: enumDef
doc:
Return definition of given enum def defined by `enumMeta`
This call forces a refresh of the definitions.

name: recTrash
doc:
Record trash action.
This function implicitly operates on all contained recs.
NOTE: this function should only be used for actions.

name: ioWriteTurtle
doc:
Write an Axon data structure to RDF [Turtle]`docHaystack::Rdf` format.
The 'val' must be an Axon type that can be converted to a Grid.

name: thisQuarter
doc: DateSpan for this 3 month quarter

name: installCancel
doc: Attempt to cancel current install pipeline

name: arcNew
doc:
Create a new arc.  Return new arc document as grid.
See `ext-arc::doc#axon`.

name: removeMarker
doc: Get the remove value singleton `haystack::Remove.val`

name: hisMapSlices
doc:
Given a source history grid and a grid of windows, compute the
mapping of each slice.  For each window in the widows grid,
the `hisSlice` is computed and passed to the function as '(grid)'.
The function must return a dict for resulting row or null to
skip mapping that window/slice.

See [Windows and Slices]`ext-hisKit::doc#windowsAndSlices`

name: hisDurProfile
doc:
Compute a "load duration" curve.  The input must be a history
grid with a 'ts' DateTime column and all the values must be
numbers with a uniform unit.  The result is a grid shaped as
follows:

   bucket   v0            v1
   ------   -----------   ----------
     a      dur v0 >= a   dur v1 >= a  ...
     b      dur v0 >= b   dur v1 >= b  ...
   ....

The function creates 100 buckets based on the min/max range
of all the values contained by the input grid.  The 'ts' column
is replaced by a 'bucket' column which defines 100 discrete
"buckets" of the value range.  The input's value columns and meta
remain unchanged.  The cells of the value columns define the number
of hours the value is greater than or equal to the bucket value.

By default gaps in the data over 1hour are ignored.  If
the 'hisInterval' grid.meta is specified and it is over 1hr,
then it is used as the gap threshold.

name: connTraceEnable
doc:
Enable tracing on the given connector.
The 'conn' parameter can be anything acceptable by `toRecId()`.

name: echo
doc: Write the str represenation of 'x' to stdout and return 'x'.

name: merge
doc:
Merge two Dicts together and return a new Dict.  Any tags
in 'b' are added to 'a'.  If 'b' defines a tag already
in 'a', then it is overwritten by 'b'.  If a tag in 'b' is
mapped to 'Remove.val', then that tag is removed from the
result.

name: folioEmptyTrash
doc:
Delete all the records marked with `trash` tag.
See `skyarcd::Proj.emptyTrash`

name: isLeapYear
doc: Return if a year is a leap year. Year must be four digit Number such as 2020.

name: geoUsStateName
doc:
Given a two letter postal code or state name, return a US state name.
If the state is not recognized, then return null or throw exception
based on checked flag.

name: isDigit
doc:
Is number a digit in the specified radix.  A decimal radix of
ten returns true for 0-9.  A radix of 16 also returns true
for a-f and A-F.

Examples:
isDigit("5".get(0))      >>  true
isDigit("A".get(0))      >>  false
isDigit("A".get(0), 16)  >>  true

name: weatherReadLocs
doc:
Search for matching weather stations given a city name.
Resulting grid contains the following columns if available:
 - `dis`: full display name to use for prompt
 - 'disRec': best display name for weather rec (city, state, country)
 - `geoCity`: city name of station
 - `geoState`: region or US state code
 - `geoCountry`: ISO 3166-1 two letter code
 - `geoCoord`: longitude,latitude of station/city
 - `tz`: best guess for timezone

Side effects:
  - performs blocking network IO

name: toList
doc: If val is a list return it, otherwise return '[val]'.

name: haystackHisRead
doc:
Perform Haystack REST API "hisRead" call to read history
data for the record identified by the id (must be Ref).
The range is any string encoding supported by the REST API
or any value supported by `toDateSpan`.  Return results
as grid with "ts" and "val" column.

name: haystackCall
doc:
Perform Haystack HTTP API call to given Str op name and with
given request grid (can be anything acceptable `toGrid`).  If
the checked flag is true and server returns an error grid, then
raise `haystack::CallErr`, otherwise return the grid itself.
Result is returned as Grid.  Also see `haystack::Client.call`.

name: xstr
doc: Construct decoded `haystack::XStr` instance

name: ioWriteLines
doc: Write a list of string lines separated with "\n" character.

name: start
doc: Start value of a DateSpan, Span or a range.

name: taskIsRunning
doc: Is the current context running asynchrounsly inside a task

name: pointWrite
doc:
Set a writable point's priority array value at the given level.
The point may be any value accepted by `toRec`.  Level must
be 1 to 17 (where 17 represents def value).  The who parameter
is a string which represent debugging information about which
user or application is writing to this priorirty array level.
If who is omitted, then the current user's display string is used

name: uriScheme
doc: Get the scheme of a Uri as a string or null

name: futureGet
doc:
Block current thread until a future's result is ready.  A null
timeout will block forever.  If an exception was raised by the
asynchronous computation, then it is raised to the caller.
See `lib-task::doc#futures`.

name: ruleTuning
doc:
Return the effective tuning Dict for the given target/rule pair.
Both target and rule may be anything accepted by `toRecId()`.

Examples:
  ruleTuning(@targetId, @ruleId)
  read(ahu).ruleTuning(read(rule and dis=="My Rule"))

name: watchAdd
doc: Add a grid of recs to an existing watch and return the grid passed in.

name: replicaBackups
doc:
List the local replica backups on this machine.
See `docSkySpark::Replication#backups`.

name: isDict
doc: Return if an object is a dict type

name: hisTransitionWindows
doc:
Compute a grid of timestamp/duration windows based on transitions
of a history grid.  The window duration is defined by the 'dur'
parameter.  The transitions are determined by the 'fn' parameter
which is a function of '(row, row)' which returns trues to create
a transition window.

The 'dur' parameter may be one of the following:
  Dur Value             Window
  ---------             -------
  Positive Number       ts .. ts+dur
  Negative Number       ts+dur .. ts
  Range of two Numbers  ts+dur.start .. ts+dur.end

In all cases above the numbers must have a duration unit (hour is
standard).  The timestamp uses is always the later of the rows being
compared.  Here are some examples assuming transition time of '2:00':

   15min          >>  2:00 to 2:15  // postive number
   -15min         >>  1:45 to 2:00  // negative number
   -20min..20min  >>  1:40 to 2:20  // range number
   -1hr..-30min   >>  1:00 to 1:30  // window before transition
    1hr..2hr      >>  3:00 to 4:00  // window after transition

Example:

  // input data (dates ignored)
  ts, v0
  00:00, false
  08:00, true
  17:00, false
  19:00, true
  20:00, false

  // function to create transitions for the 30min window
  // after each transition from true => false
  hisTransitionWindows(src, 30min) (a,b) => a->v0 and not b->v0

  // resulting grid of windows
  ts, v0
  17:00, 0.5hr
  20:00, 0.5hr

See [Windows and Slices]`ext-hisKit::doc#windowsAndSlices`

name: diff
doc:
Construct a modification "diff" used by `commit`.  The orig should
be the instance which was read from the database, or it may be null
only if the add flag is passed.  Any tags to add/set/remove should
be included in the changes dict.

The following flags are supported:
- 'add': indicates diff is adding new record
- 'remove': indicates diff is removing record (in general you
should add `trash` tag instead of removing)
- 'transient': indicate that this diff should not be flushed
to persistent storage (it may or may not be persisted).
- 'force': indicating that changes should be applied regardless
of other concurrent changes which may be been applied after
the orig version was read (use with caution!)

Examples:
// create new record
diff(null, {dis:"New Rec", someMarker}, {add})

// create new record with explicit id like Diff.makeAdd
diff(null, {id:151bd3c5-6ce3cb21, dis:"New Rec"}, {add})

// set/add dis tag and remove oldTag
diff(orig, {dis:"New Dis", -oldTag})

// set/add val tag transiently
diff(orig, {val:123}, {transient})

name: pow
doc: Return val raised to the specified power.

name: energyNormByDegreeDay
doc:
Given a his grid, normalize the values by heating or cooling degree-day.
Each value column must have a `siteRef` which references a site with
the `weatherStationRef` tag.

name: fold
doc:
Fold a list or stream into a single value using given folding function.
The folding function signature must be '(val, acc)' where val is the items
being folded, and acc is an accumulator used to maintain
state between iterations.  Lifecycle of a fold:
1. Call 'fn(foldStart, null)', return initial accumulator state
2. Call 'fn(item, acc)' for every item, return new accumulator state
3. Call 'fn(foldEnd, acc)' return final result

See `docHaxall::Streams#fold` for streaming details.

The fold will short-circuit and halt immediately if the folding
function returns `na()` for the accumulator state. The result of
the fold is `na()` in this case.  A folding function should document
its behavior when a collection contains `na()`.

Built-in folding functions include:
- `count()`
- `sum()`
- `avg()`
- `min()`
- `max()`
- `mean()`
- `median()`
- `rootMeanSquareErr()`
- `meanBiasErr()`
- `standardDeviation()`

Examples:
[1, 2, 3, 4].fold(max)  // fold list into its max value
[1, 2, 3, 4].fold(avg)  // fold list into its average value
[1, 2, na(), 3].fold(sum) // => na()

Example of writing your own custom fold function that
used start/end values and has support for na():
average: (val, acc) => do
if (val == foldStart()) return {sum:0, count:0}
if (val == foldEnd()) return acc->sum / acc->count
if (val == na()) return na()
return {sum: acc->sum + val, count: acc->count + 1}
end
Also see `reduce()` which is easier to use if doing your
own simple rollup computation.

name: provDownloadBuild
doc:
Download a SkySpark build from skyfoundry.com and create or update a
[provBuild]`ext-prov::doc#provBuild` manifest for it. This also downloads
all current patches for the build and creates or updates a
[provPatch]`ext-prov::doc#provPatch` for it.

  provDownloadBuild("3.0.22")

The 'opts' parameters supports the following options:

- 'excludePaths':('Uri[,]') List of relative path URIs for files to exclude
from the build when creating the manifest.

pre>
provDownloadBuild("3.0.22", {excludePaths:[`lib/fan/hvacExt.pod`]})
<pre

name: geoCountryName
doc:
Given a two ISO-3166 country code or country name, return a country name.
If the country is not recognized, then return null or throw exception
based on checked flag.

name: queryAll
doc:
Evaluate a relationship query and return grid of results.
Subject must be a record id or dict in the database.  Spec
must be a Spec typed as a 'sys::Query'.  Also see `query`.

Options:
- 'limit': max number of recs to return
- 'sort': sort by display name
Example:
read(ahu).queryAll(spec("ph::Equip.points"))

name: contains
doc:
Return if 'val' contains 'x':
- if 'val' is Str, then 'x' is substring.
- if 'val' is List, then 'x' is item to search.
- if 'val' is Range, then is 'x' inside the range inclusively
- if 'val' is DateSpan, then is 'x' a date in the span

name: toTimeZone
doc:
Convert a DateTime or Span to another timezone:
now().toTimeZone("Chicago")
now().toTimeZone("UTC")

name: specLib
doc:
Load or lookup a Xeto library by its string or ref name.  Return
the [dict]`xeto::Lib` representation.  If not found raise
exception or return null based on checked flag.

Examples:
specLib("ph.points")            // load by dotted name
specLib(@lib:ph.points)         // load by lib ref id
specLib("bad.lib.name")         // raises exception if not found
specLib("bad.lib.name", false)  // unchecked returns null

name: sysModAdd
doc:
Enable the given system module name such as "ldap".
All dependencies must be already enabled.

name: uriEncode
doc:
Return the percent encoded string for this Uri according to RFC 3986.
Each section of the Uri is UTF-8 encoded into octects and then percent
encoded according to its valid character set. Spaces in the query
section are encoded as '+'.

Examples:
`foo bar`.uriEncode  >>  "foo%20bar"

name: has
doc:
If val is a Grid return if it has the given column name.
If val is a Dict return if the given name is mapped to a non-null value.

name: ioGzip
haystackReadById
doc:
Perform Haystack HTTP API call to read a record by its unique
identifier.  Return result as dict.  If the record is not found, then
return null or raise UnknownRecErr based on checked flag.  Also
see `haystack::Client.readById`.

name: times
doc:
Call the specified function the given number
of times passing the counter.

name: isGrid
doc: Return if an object is a grid type

name: toOccupied
doc:
Given a `site`, `space`, `equip`, or `point` rec, get its `occupied`
point.  The following algorithm is used to lookup the occupied point:
1. Try to find in equip or parent of nested equip
2. Try to find in space or parent of nested spaces
3. Try to find in site if site if tagged as `sitePoint`

If there are no matches or multiple ambiguous matches, then return
null or raise an exception based on checked flag.

name: matrixDeterminant
doc:
Return the determinant as a unitless Number for the given matrix which
is any value accepted by `toMatrix`.  The matrix must be square.

name: instance
doc:
Load or lookup a instance from a Xeto library by its string or ref qname
as a Dict. If not found raise exception or return null based on checked flag.

NOTE: this function returns the instance using Haystack level fidelity

Examples:
instance("icons::apple")             // qname string
instance(@icons::apple)              // qname Ref id
instance("icons::bad-name")          // raises exception if not found
instance("icons::bad-name", false)   // unchecked returns null

name: ruleOrphans
doc:
Search the database for all previously cached sparks and KPIs which no
longer have a valid rule record.  The targets parameter may be any expr
accepted by `toRecList()` or null for all.  Span is any expr accepted
by `toSpan()`.  This situation occurs when you delete rule records from
the database which have already cached their results.  Or this function
will also report targets which no longer match an existing rule's ruleOn
filter.

The result is a grid with a row for each unique target+rule combination.
The grid has following columns:
  - targetRef: target id
  - ruleRef: orphaned rule id
  - ruleType: "spark" or "kpi"
  - numDates: number of dates with orphaned sparks or kpis

You may pipe the results of this function directly to `ruleDelete()`
to delete the orphaned data.

Also see the [documentation]`ext-rule::doc#orphans`.

Examples:
  // run narrow report on specific targets and span
  readAll(ahu).ruleOrphans(thisMonth)

  // report on all orphaned data across database
  ruleOrphans(null, null)

  // delete all orphans across all targets and all time
  ruleOrphans(null, null).ruleDelete

name: xqProjs
doc:
Restrict query to given project name(s) - see `skyarcd::XQuery.projs`.
Also see `navProjNames` to restrict a query to navigable projects.

name: decapitalize
doc:
Return this string with the first character converted to
lowercase.  The case conversion is for ASCII only.
Also see `capitalize()` and `lower()`.

Examples:
decapitalize("Apple") >> "apple"

name: specIs
doc:
Return if spec 'a' inherits from spec 'b' based on nominal typing.
This method checks the explicit inheritance hierarchy via `specBase()`.
Use `is()` to check if an instance is of a given type.  Also see
`fits()` and `specFits()` to check using structural typing.

Examples:
specIs(Str, Scalar)     >>  true
specIs(Scalar, Scalar)  >>  false
specIs(Equip, Dict)     >>  true
specIs(Meter, Equip)    >>  true
specIs(Meter, Point)    >>  false

name: matrixInverse
doc: Return the inverse of the given matrix which is any value accepted by `toMatrix`.

name: remove
doc:
Remove an item from a collection and return a new collection.
- List: key is index to remove at
- Dict: key is tag name

name: favTrash
doc:
Trash a favorite record.
Do not use this function directly; for actions only.

name: ioToHex
doc: Encode an I/O handle into hexidecimal string.

name: ioZipEntry
doc:
Return a I/O handle which may be used to read from a zip
entry within a zip file.  The 'handle' parameter must be
an I/O handle which references a zip file in the file system.
The 'path' parameter must be a Uri which identifies the
path of the entry within the zip file.  See `ioZipDir` to
read the listing of paths within a zip.

Example:
// read CSV file from within a zip
ioZipEntry(`io/batch.zip`, `/zone-temp.csv`).ioReadCsv

name: addCols
doc:
Add grid b as a new set of columns to grid a.  If b contains
duplicate column names, then they are given auto-generated
unique names.  If b contains fewer rows then a, then the missing
cells are filled with null.

Examples:
[{a:0, b:2}, {a:1, b:3}].toGrid.addCols({c:4}.toGrid)
readAll(rtu).addCols(readAll(meter))

name: isAlphaNum
doc:
Is number an ASCII alpha-numeric char: isAlpha||isDigit

Examples:
isAlphaNum("A".get(0))  >>  true
isAlphaNum("a".get(0))  >>  true
isAlphaNum("8".get(0))  >>  true
isAlphaNum(" ".get(0))  >>  false
isAlphaNum("Ã".get(0))  >>  false

name: isWatched
doc:
Return if given record is under at least one watch.
The rec argument can be any value accepted by `toRecId()`.

name: sysModRemove
doc:
Disable the given system module name.  Raise exception
if it is a boot module or it is required for dependencies.

name: skip
doc:
Skip the given number of items in a stream.
See `docHaxall::Streams#skip`.

name: meanBiasErr
doc:
Fold a sample of numbers into their MBE (mean bias error).
The MBE function determines the MBE between a sample set and
its mean:

MBE = Σ(xᵢ - median) / (n - nDegrees)

Examples:
samples.fold(meanBiasErr)         // unbiased zero degrees of freedom
samples.fold(meanBiasErr(_,_,1))  // 1 degree of freedom

name: toEquips
doc:
Map a set of recs to to a grid of [equips]`equip`.  The 'recs'
parameter may be any value accepted by `toRecList()`.  Return empty
grid if no mapping is found.  The following mappings are supported:
- recs with 'equip' tag are mapped as themselves
- recs with 'equipRef' tag are mapped to their parent equip
- recs with 'site' are mapped to equip with parent 'siteRef'
- recs with 'space' are mapped to equip with parent 'spaceRef'

Examples:
read(site).toEquips      // return children equip within site
read(space).toEquips     // return children equip within space
read(equip).toEquips     // return equip itself
read(point).toEquips     // return point's parent equip

name: logE
doc: Return natural logarithm to the base e of val.

name: ioToBase64
doc:
Encode an I/O handle into a base64 string.  The default behavior
is to encode using RFC 2045 (see `sys::Buf.toBase64`).  Use the '{uri}'
option to encode a URI-safe URI via RFC 4648 (see `sys::Buf.toBase64Uri`).
Also see `ioFromBase64`.

Example:
// encode string to base64
ioToBase64("myusername:mysecret")

// encode string to base64 without padding using URI safe chars
ioToBase64("myusername:mysecret", {uri})

name: userCur
doc: Read user rec Dict for current session user

name: nowTicks
doc:
Return current time as nanosecond ticks since 1 Jan 2000 UTC.
Note that the 64-bit floating point representations of nanosecond
ticks will lose accuracy below the microsecond.  Also see `now()`.

name: reduce
doc:
Reduce a collection to a single value with the given reducer
function.  The given function is called with each item in the
collection along with a current *accumulation* value.  The accumation
value is initialized to 'init' for the first item, and for every
subsequent item it is the result of the previous item.  Return
the final accumulation value.  Also see `fold` which is preferred
if doing standard rollup such as sum or average.

If working with a list, the function takes '(acc, val, index)'
and returns accumulation value

If working with a grid, the function takes '(acc, row, index)'
and returns accumulation value

If working with a stream, then function takes '(acc, val)'
and returns accumulation value  See `docHaxall::Streams#reduce`.

Examples:
[2, 5, 3].reduce(0, (acc, val)=>acc+val)  >> 10
[2, 5, 3].reduce(1, (acc, val)=>acc*val)  >> 30

name: ioWriteExcel
doc:
Write an Excel XLS file, where 'val' may be:
- Grid - writted a a single worksheet
- Grid[] - each grid is exported as a separate worksheet

By default each worksheet is named "Sheet1", "Sheet2", etc.  Use
a 'title' tag in Grid.meta to give the worksheets a specific name.

Example:
readAll(site).ioWriteExcel(`io/sites.xls`)

name: arcNewPart
doc:
Add a new part to an arc record.  Orig must be an arc record and part
must be a dict with the new part tags.  Return new arc document as grid.
This function requires current user to have "newPart" permission.
See `ext-arc::doc#axon`.

name: readCount
doc:
Return the number of records which match the given filter expression.

Examples:
readCount(point)    // return number of recs with point tag

name: hisCollectWriteAll
doc:
Write all hisCollect items buffered in memory to the historian.
Block until complete or until timeout exceeded.

name: setMeta
doc:
Return new grid with grid level meta-data replaced by given
meta Dict.  Also see `addMeta` and `docHaxall::Streams#setMeta`.

Example:
read(temp).hisRead(today).setMeta({view:"table"})

name: clamp
doc:
Clamp the number val between the min and max.  If its less than min
then return min, if its greater than max return max, otherwise return
val itself.  The min and max must have matching units or be unitless.
The result is always in the same unit as val.

Examples:
14.clamp(10, 20)     >>   14    // is between 10 and 20
3.clamp(10, 20)      >>   10    // clamps to min 10
73.clamp(10, 20)     >>   20    // clamps to max 20
45°F.clamp(60, 80)   >>   60°F  // min/max can be unitless

name: isDateTime
doc: Return if an object is a DateTime type

name: pointEmergencyOverride
doc:
User level-1 manual override of writable point.
See `pointWrite`.

name: specOf
doc:
Return the Xeto spec of the given value.  Raise exception
if value type is not mapped into the data type system.  Also
see `is()` and `fits()`.

Examples:
specOf("hi")                 >>  sys::Str
specOf(@id)                  >>  sys::Ref
specOf({})                   >>  sys::Dict
specOf({spec:@ph::Equip})    >>  ph::Dict

name: abs
doc: Return absolute value of a number, if null return null

name: terms
doc: List term definitions (tags and conjuncts) in the context namespace as Def[].

name: median
doc:
Fold a sample of numbers into their median value which is the
middle value of the sorted samples.  If there are an even number
of sample, then the median is the mean of the middle two.  Null
values are ignored.  Return null if no values.

Example:
[2, 4, 5, 3, 1].fold(median)

name: geoTz
doc:
Given a dict/row that contains primary geo tags such as `geoCountry`,
`geoState`, and `geoCity` return ideal timezone name.

NOTE: if your city or country is not correctly modeled by this
 function then please let us know and we can update our database.

name: hisRollup
doc:
Roll up a history grid over discrete intervals of time with
the given fold function.  The src must be a history grid created
from `hisRead` or some transformation of it.

Rollups are inclusive of start and exclusive of end times.
For example a daily rollup includes midnight of the current day,
but excludes midnight of the next day. All the items found within
the interval are passed to the folding function to compute a
timestamp/val pair with each timestamp aligned to the start of the
interval.  Use the string "*" to rollup the entire grid as one
big interval.

For example a rollup interval of 1day over a one year range
would calculate 365 (or 366 if leap year) items with the
start time being midnight of each day. Interval rules:
 - Less 1day: use absolute time, aligned off start (example 1hr)
 - 1day: daily rollup, aligned off midnight
 - 1wk: weekly rollup, aligned off midnight on Sunday
 - 1mo: monthly rollup, aligned off midnight on 1st of month
 - 3mo: quarterly rollup, aligned off midnight on 1st of 3 month intervals
 - 1yr: yearly rollup, aligned off midnight Jan 1st
 - Anything else is an error

See 'core::fold' for how folding functions work.  History rollups
also support rollup functions with arity of 3 '(val, ts, acc)'.

This function will work with [forecast data]`ext-his::doc#forecast` provided
one of the following is true:
 1. You did the initial `hisRead()` with the '{forecast}' option
 1. The column metadata for the value has a `hisEnd` in the future
 1. The data comes from a computed point (`hisFunc`)
 1. The data does not come from a demo database

name: renameCol
doc:
Return a new grid with the given column renamed.

Example:
readAll(site).renameCol("dis", "title")

name: taskProgress
doc:
Update the current running task's progress data with given dict.
This is a silent no-op if the current context is not running in a task.

Example:
// report progress percentage processing a list of records
recs.each((rec, index)=>do
taskProgress({percent: round(100%*index/recs.size), cur:rec.dis})
processRec(rec)
end)
taskProgress({percent:100%})

name: ioAppend
doc:
Convert a handle to append mode.  Writes will append to the end
of the file instead of rewriting the file.  Raises UnsupportedErr
if the handle doesn't support append mode.

Example:
ioWriteStr("append a line\n", ioAppend(`io/foo.txt`))

name: add
doc: Add item to the end of a list and return a new list.

name: feed
doc:
Convert a stream into a live feed to populate a view.
See `docHaxall::Streams#feed`

name: weekday
doc:
Get weekday as integer from 0 to 6 of Date or DateTime.
Zero indicates Sunday and 6 indicates Saturday

name: validateUsername
doc: Raise an excpetion if string is not a validate username

name: ruleToTargets
doc:
Get all the target records the rule is registered on by the `ruleOn`
tag.  The rule may be any value accepted by `toRec`.

name: watchPoll
doc:
Poll an open watch and return all the records which have changed
since the last poll.  Raise exception if watchId doesn't exist
or has expired.  Also see `hx::HxWatch.poll` and `docHaxall::Watches#axon`.

name: ruleTunings
doc: Read grid of tuning records sorted from lowest to highest priority.

name: favEdit
doc:
Edit a favotie record.
Do not use this function directly; for actions only.

name: xqHisRead
doc: Read point history data - see `skyarcd::XQuery.hisRead`

name: log10
doc: Return base 10 logarithm of val.

name: degreeDaysBase
doc:
Given a `site` or `weather` record, return the default balance point
temperature to use for `degreeDays`.  This function can return a single
number to use for both the heating and cooling balance point, or it can
return a dict with separate balance points using the tags "heat" and "cool".
The number(s) returned must have an explicit unit of either °C or °F.
The default implementation is 18°C or 65°F. Also see `isMetric`.

Examples:
  65°F                    // single balance point
  {heat:17°C, cool:19°C}  // separate balance points

name: futureWaitForAll
doc:
Block on a list of futures until they all transition to a completed
state.  If timeout is null block forever, otherwise raise TimeoutErr
if any one of the futures does not complete before the timeout elapses.
See `lib-task::doc#futures`.

name: ioStreamLines
doc:
Read a stream of lines.  Lines are processed according to
`sys::InStream.eachLine`.  See `docHaxall::Streams#ioStreamLines`.

name: parseDate
doc:
Parse a Str into a Date.  If the string cannot be parsed into a valid
Date and checked is false then return null, otherwise throw ParseErr.
See `sys::Date.toLocale` for pattern.

Examples:
parseDate("7-Feb-23", "D-MMM-YY")
parseDate("07/02/23", "DD/MM/YY")
parseDate("7 february 2023", "D MMMM YYYY")
parseDate("230207", "YYMMDD")

name: endsWith
doc:
Return if Str ends with the specified Str.

Examples:
"hi there".endsWith("there")   >>  true
"hi there".endsWith("hi")      >>  false

name: foldStart
doc: The fold start marker value

name: taskCancel
doc:
Set cancel flag for the given task.  Cancelling a task sets an
internal flag which is checked by the context's heartbeat on every
Axon call.  On the next Axon call the current message context
will raise a `sys::CancelledErr` which will be raised by the respective
future.  Cancelling a task does **not** interrupt any current operations,
so any blocking future or I/O calls should always use a timeout.

name: index
doc:
Return the first match of 'x' in 'val' searching forward, starting
at the specified offset index.  A negative offset may be used to
access from the end of string.  Return null if no occurences are found:
- if 'val' is Str, then 'x' is substring.
- if 'val' is List, then 'x' is item to search.

name: all
doc:
Return if all the items in a list, dict, or grid match the
given test function.  If the collection is empty, then return
true.

If working with a list, the function takes '(val)'
or '(val, index)' and returns true or false.

If working with a dict, the function takes '(val)'
or '(val, name)' and returns true or false.

If working with a grid, the function takes '(row)'
or '(row, index)' and returns true or false.

If working with a string, the function takes '(char)'
or '(char, index)' and returns true or false.

If working with a stream, then function takes '(val)'
and returns true or false.  See `docHaxall::Streams#all`.

Examples:
[1, 3, 5].all v => v.isOdd  >>  true
[1, 3, 6].all(isOdd)        >>  false

name: renameTags
doc:
Rename a set of tags on a list of records or across the entire project
database.  This function is easily paired with `deprecatedTags()` to
preview and fix standard tags which are deprecated over time.

The recs parameters specify the recs for the operation.  Or pass
null to rename every rec in the project database.

The renames parameter may be any of the following shapes:
  - Dict of old to new tag names
  - Grid with 'old' and 'new' columns

If the new value is a string, then the tag is renamed to the given
new name.  If new value is the Remove value then the tag is simply
removed (only if removeOld option passed).  If the new value is
a conjuct formatted as "foo-bar-baz", then each part is created
as a marker tag.

Summary of options:
  - commit: marker to force commit (preview by default)
  - removeOld: remove the old tag (old tag left by default)
  - limit: number of diffs (defaults to 1000)

The default behavior is to preview the changes only and to leave
the old tags in place side-by-side with the new tags.  To apply
the changes pass the 'commit' option.  To force removal of the
old tags pass the 'removeOld'.

As a saftey measure no more than 1000 diffs are previewed or
committed.  You can remove the limit by passing '-limit' in the
options.  However if removing the limit in a large project, then
both thew preview or commit results might be too large to view in
the Shell view.  In this case, pipe the results to a file
using `ioWriteZinc()`.

Examples:
  // rename of foo -> bar across all records
  // opts: preview, leave old tag, limit 1000
  renameTags(null, {foo: "bar"})

  // rename of foo -> bar across all records
  // opts: commit, remove old tag, no limit
  renameTags(null, {foo: "bar"}, {commit, removeOld, -limit})

  // remove of foo across all points
  // opts: preview, remove old tag, limit 10
  readAll(point).renameTags({-foo}, {removeOld, limit:10})

  // rename of elecMeter -> elec + meter and steamPlant -> steam + plant
  // across all equip records
  readAll(equip).renameTags({elecMeter: "elec-meter", steamPlant: "steam-plant"})

  // combine with deprecatedTags() to rename all deprecated tags
  // and pipe results to a zinc file in the io/ directory
  renameTags(null, deprecatedTags()).ioWriteZinc(`io/rename-log.zinc`)

name: xqHisRollup
doc: Rollup history data - see `skyarcd::XQuery.hisRollup`

name: eachMonth
doc:
Iterate the months of a span.  The 'dates' argument may be any object
converted into a date range by `toDateSpan`.  The given function is
called with a 'DateSpan' argument for each interated month.

Examples:
// iterate each month in 2010, and echo data range
eachMonth(2010) d => echo(d)

// call f once for current method
eachMonth(today(), f)

name: userReadAll
doc:
Read a Grid of recs from the user db by filter. If the context user
is not su, then only users with access to the to the current
context project are returned.

name: futureState
doc:
Return current state of a future as one of the following strings:
- 'pending': still queued or being processed
- 'ok': completed with result value
- 'err': completed with an exception
- 'cancelled': future was cancelled before processing
See `lib-task::doc#futures`.

name: haystackReadAll
doc:
Perform Haystack REST API call to read all entities with filter.
The filter is an expression like `readAll`.  Return results
as grid.  Also see `haystack::Client.readAll`.

name: ioExport
doc:
Export a view to the given file handle - see `docFresco::Export`.

Note: this feature is available in SkySpark only

name: standardDeviation
doc:
Fold a series of numbers into the standard deviation of a *sample*:

s = sqrt(Σ (xᵢ - mean)² / (n-1))

Example:
[4, 2, 5, 8, 6].fold(standardDeviation)

name: negInf
doc: Return the Number representation negative infinity

name: ruleKpis
doc:
Query local KPIs along three dimensions of target x dates x rules.
Targets and rules are any expr accepted by `toRecList` or null for all.
Span is any expr accepted by `toSpan`.

name: hisRemove
doc:
Remove items from a history using a timestamp or timestamp range.
This function blocks until the remove is complete.

WARNING: once the data is removed, there is **NO** recovery!!!

The hisRec supports anything accepted by `toRec`.  Note this
function only works on one record at a time.

The spans argument can be any non-null span accepted by `hisRead` or
a list of such spans.  If a single DateTime is used then just
that item is removed, otherwise any items within the span's
inclusive start time and exclusive end time are removed.  Note that
timestamps are normalized according to the record's `hisTsPrecision`.

This function is best used for fine grained removal.  Also see
the `hisClear()` function for removing multiple points or the
entire history.

The result of this function is unspecified and may change.

Examples:
  hisRemove(pt, ts)              // remove single item
  hisRemove(pt, startTs..endTs)  // remove span of items
  hisRemove(pt, [ts1, ts2])      // remove list of timestamps

name: collect
doc:
Collect stream into a in-memory list or grid.
See `docHaxall::Streams#collect`.

name: enumDefs
doc:
Return grid of current enum defs defined by `enumMeta`.
This call forces a refresh of the definitions.

name: pointAuto
doc:
User level-8 manual auto (override release) of writable point.
See `pointWrite`.

name: xobjReadAllByKey
doc:
Read all the matches for given extension name and key.  Return list of
dicts in same format as `xobjReadByKey`.

name: httpSiteUri
doc: Primary HTTP or HTTPS Uri - see `hx::HxHttpService.siteUri`

name: limit
doc:
Truncate stream after given limit is reached.
See `docHaxall::Streams#limit`.

name: extRemove
doc:
Disable the given extension name or list of names.  Raise
exception if other extensions currently depend on this one.

Examples:
  extRemove("bacnet")
  extRemove(["bacnet", "opc"])

name: ioSkip
doc:
Apply a skipping operation to an input I/O handle.  The
following options are available (in order of processing):
- bom: skip byte order mark
- bytes: number of bytes to skip (must be binary input stream)
- chars: number of chars to skip (must be text input stream)
- lines: number of lines to skip

Skipping a BOM will automatically set the appropiate charset.
If no BOM is detected, then this call is safely ignored by pushing
those bytes back into the input stream.  The following byte
order marks are supported:
- UTF-16 Big Endian: 0xFE_FF
- UTF-16 Little Endian: 0xFF_FE
- UTF-8: 0xEF_BB_BF

Examples:
// skip leading 4 lines in a CSV file
ioSkip(`io/foo.csv`, {lines:4}).ioReadCsv

// skip byte order mark
ioSkip(`io/foo.csv`, {bom}).ioReadCsv

name: hisRollupAuto
doc:
Automatically chooses the best rollup interval and the best rollup
fold function for each history.

If null is passed for the interval, then the following automatic
selection is made:
  - less 2days: Check each history rec to see if any specify the `hisRollupAutoMinInterval`
    tag and take the *maximum* value. Otherwise, no rollup is applied and the raw
    history grid is returned.
  - one week: 10min interval
  - one month: 1hour interval
  - over one month: daily interval

The rollup function applied to each history is determined as follows:
  1. if foldFinder parameter is specified, it is called for each
     history to get fold function.  If it returns null we check next
     step
  2. if `hisRollupFunc` is defined on the history, then use it
  3. Select fold function based on kind:
     a. Bool: `discreteBoolPeriods`
     b. Number with `hisMode` of "cov": `covAvg`
     c. Number pick based on unit's quantity:
       - power: `max`
       - volume, energy: `sum`
       - default: `avg`
     d. Str with enum tag: `discreteEnumPeriods`
     e. fallback to `count`

If your history has no data or is a computed history, you should
explicitly specify the `kind` or `hisRollupFunc` to ensure a suitable
rollup is selected.

If a rollup is applied, then the following tags are automatically
applied to the resulting grid:
  - 'hisRollupInterval': applied to grid.meta interval used for the rollup
  - 'hisRollup': applied to col.meta of each value column (such as "avg")
  - 'hisRollupDis': applied to col.meta for each rollup, if all the columns
    have the same rollup, then it is applied to grid.meta also

Examples:
  // over 1hr intervals
  src.hisRead(pastWeek).hisRollupAuto

  // over 6hr intervals
  src.hisRead(pastMonth).hisRollupAuto

  // explicit 1day intervals
  src.hisRead(pastMonth).hisRollupAuto(1day)

  // explicit fold finder, uses max for all columns
  src.hisRead(pastMonth).hisRollupAuto(null, his => max)

name: ruleDelete
doc:
Delete works just like `ruleRecompute` except it also deletes
the sparks/KPIs from the cache.  This function can be very expensive
compared to a simple recompute.  Recompute only requires updating
the index to reset specific rule/date combinations to the unknown
state.  This function does the same, but also removes the actual
cached spark/KPI for each rule/date combination.

This function deletes the sparks synchronously and can take a long
time if there are many targets.  If you deleting many targets then
run this function within a task.

The default behavior is to automatically call `ruleRosterRefresh` before
the recompute to ensure the rules/targets are up-to-date.  This behavior
can be overridden by passing '{noRefreshRoster}' for options.

This function will also accept the result of the `ruleOrphans()` report.

name: addCol
doc:
Add a column to a grid by mapping each row to a new cell value.
The 'col' parameter may be a simple String name or may be a
dictionary which must have a "name" tag (any other
tags become column meta-data).  The mapping function takes
'(row)' and returns the new cell values for the column.

Examples:
// add new column named areaMeter
readAll(site).addCol("areaMeters") s => s->area.to(1m²)

// add new column named areaMeter with dis meta
readAll(site).addCol({name:"areaMeters", dis:"Area Meters"}) s => s->area.to(1m²)

name: connSyncHis
doc:
Perform a remote sync of history data for the given points.
The 'points' parameter may be anything acceptable by `toRecIdList()`.
The 'span' parameter is anything acceptable by `toSpan()`.  Or pass
null for span to perform a sync for items after the point's `hisEnd`.
This blocks the calling thread until each point is synchronized one
by one.  Normally it should only be called within a task.  The result
from this function is undefined.  Also see `docHaxall::Conns#hisSync`.

Examples:
readAll(haystackHis).connSyncHis(null)

name: pointDetails
doc:
Get debug string for point including writables and his collection.
The argument is anything acceptable by `toRec()`.
The result is returned as a plain text string.

Examples:
read(dis=="My Point").pointDetails
pointDetails(@2b80f96a-820a4f1a)

name: ioWriteHtml
doc:
Write an Axon data structure to HTML. The 'val' must be an
Axon type that can be converted to a Grid.

name: userBackupCleanup
doc: Delete all user database backups older than the given duration or date.

name: hisFindPeriods
doc:
Find all the periods of time when 'fn' returns true for a history
grid.  The filter func takes '(val, ts, his)' and returns true
or false. This function only works on data up to the current time; it does
not work with future data.

By default, empty columns will be removed. To keep empty columns you may
do one of the following:
  1. pass the 'hisKeepEmptyCols' option to `hisRead`
  1. add the 'hisKeepEmptyCols' marker tag to the history grid meta
     using `addMeta`

name: year
doc: Get year as integer such as 2010 from date or datetime

name: toSites
doc:
Map a set of recs to to a grid of [sites]`site`.  The 'recs'
parameter may be any value accepted by `toRecList()`.  Return empty
grid if no mapping is found.  The following mappings are supported:
- recs with 'site' tag are mapped as themselves
- recs with 'siteRef' tag are mapped to their parent site

Examples:
read(site).toSites     // return site itself
read(space).toSites    // return space's parent site
read(equip).toSites    // return equip's parent site
read(point).toSites    // return point's parent site

name: toChar
doc:
Convert a unicode char number into a single char string

Examples:
toChar(65)   >>  "A"

name: arcDigest
doc:
Format a grid of arc summary records for a [notification]`lib:notify`.
If 'since' parameter is a span, then all arcs and parts that have been
modified in that span are returned. Otherwise, if 'since' is a DateTime,
then all arcs and parts from that time until now are returned.

  // read digest for all arcs which have been updated since midnight
  readAll(arc).arcDigest(dateTime(today(), 0:00))

name: ceil
doc:
Return the smallest whole number greater than or equal to val.
Result has same unit as 'val'.

name: isRef
doc: Return if an object is a ref type

name: ioInfo
doc:
Get information about a file handle and return a Dict with the
same tags as `ioDir()`.

If the I/O handle does not map to a file in the virtual file system then
throw an exception.

ioInfo(`io/`)            // read file info for the project's io/ directory
ioInfo(`io/sites.trio`)  // read file info for the io/sites.trio file

name: lower
doc:
Convert a char number or str to ASCII lower case.
Also see `upper()` and `decapitalize()`.

Examples:
lower("CAT")      >>  "cat"
lower("Cat")      >>  "cat"
lower(65).toChar  >>  "a"

name: specName
doc:
Return simple name of spec.

Examples:
specName(Dict)  >>  "Dict"
specName(Site)  >>  "Site"

name: uiLinkAbs
doc:
Convenience for `httpSiteUri()` + `uiLink()` to generate an
absolute URI hyperlink

name: covAvg
doc:
Folding function for generating a time weighted average
on change-of-value histories (where `hisInterpolate` is "cov").
The average is calcualted by weighting each sample with
its ratio of duration in the the overall interval.  For
example given the following time/value pairs:

  00:00  10
  02:00  30
  12:00  70

If performing a 1day rollup with the data above, the point
had a value of 10 for 2 hours, a value of 30 for ten hours,
and a value of 70 for 12 hours (until end of day).  The
time weighted COV average would be 48.3:
  10 * (2/24) + 30 * (10/24) + 70 * (12/24)

If any value in the rollup interval is `na()`, then the
result is `na()`.

name: ioStreamCsv
doc:
Read a stream of dicts from a comma separated value file.  This
function uses the same options and semantics as `ioReadCsv` except
it streams the rows as dicts instead of reading to an in-memory grid.
See `docHaxall::Streams#ioStreamCsv`.

name: folioBackupCleanup
doc:
Delete all backups older than the given duration or date.
Requires admin permission.

Examples:
  folioBackupCleanup(15day)       // delete all backups older than 15 days
  folioBackupCleanup(2017-11-15)  // delete all backups before given date

name: userMfaStatus
doc:
Check if MFA is required and if user is enrolled.

Examples:
pre>
// check a user MFA status
userMfaStatus(@userId)

userReadAll(<filter>).userMfaStatus
<pre

name: install
doc:
Asynchronously install the given pods which is a Dict or list
of Dicts specifying the repo name, pod name and version.
Example:
  install({repo:"StackHub", name:"foo", version:"1.0.3"})

name: insert
doc: Insert an item into a list at the given index and return a new list.

name: provSkySparkImage
doc:
Create a [provImage]`ext-prov::doc#provImage` for the given SkySpark build number.
It will contain that build and the current patches. You may optionally pass an
*ordered* list of additional manifests to include (append) to the image.

  provSkySparkImage("3.0.22")

  provSkySparkImage("3.0.22", [@myOverlay1, @myOverlay2])

name: xqUseReplicas
doc: Route to replicas instead of master - see `skyarcd::XQuery.useReplicas`.

name: tzdb
doc:
Return the installed timezone database as Grid with following columns:
- name: name of the timezone
- fullName: qualified name used by Olson database

name: toPoints
doc:
Map a set of recs to to a grid of [points]`point`.  The 'recs'
parameter may be any value accepted by `toRecList()`.  Return empty
grid if no mapping is found.  The following mappings are supported:
- recs with 'point' tag are mapped as themselves
- recs with 'site' are mapped to points with parent 'siteRef'
- recs with 'space' are mapped to points with parent 'spaceRef'
- recs with 'equip' are mapped to points with parent 'equipRef'
- recs with 'device' are mapped to points with parent 'deviceRef'

Examples:
read(site).toPoints      // return children points within site
read(space).toPoints     // return children points within space
read(equip).toPoints     // return children points within equip
read(device).toPoints    // return children points within device

name: logErr
doc: Log at the "err" level - see `log`.

name: xqReadByIdsList
doc: Read recs by ids - see `skyarcd::XQuery.readByIds`

name: nowUtc
doc:
Return current DateTime in UTC.  This function will use a  cached
version which is only accurate to within 250ms (see `sys::DateTime.nowUtc`
for details).  Also see `now()` and `nowTicks()`.

name: navResolve
doc:
Resolve a list of navigation Refs to a list of Dict records.
Also see `docFresco::Nav#navSelAny`.

Default behavior is to resolve using only the local project.  Pass
the 'navCluster' marker option to resolve across the cluster based
on the [projIncludeFilter]`docFresco::Nav#projIncludeFilter`
configuration.

Navigation [nesting]`docFresco::Nav#nesting` is when a given level
of the tree is organized using a reference tag.  For example 'equip'
recs are nested by the 'equipRef' tag.  The default behavior of
this method is to **not** apply nesting to compute children of
of a navigation ref.  However this behavior can be overridden by
passing the 'nestExpand' marker tag.  This option is *extremely*
expensive and should be avoided if at all possible (especially if
combined with 'navCluster' option).  Examples:

  // nested tree of equip via equipRef
  eq
    eqA
      eqA1
      eqA2
    eqB

  // default yields only [eq]
  navRef("equip", eq).navResolve

  // yields [eq, eqA, eqA1, eqA2, eqB] (eq and its nested children)
  navRef("equip", eq).navResolve({nestExpand})

Note the nestExpand feature only works at the leaf level.  In the
example above the target name is "equip", so we can expand 'eq' to
include its nested children.  However if target was "point", then we
would **not** expand 'eq' to include the points from 'eqA', 'eqA1', 'eqA2',
and 'eqB'.

Option summary:
  - 'kind': pass in kind value string used in input
  - 'navCluster': pass marker to resolve across cluster
  - 'nestExpand': pass marker to expand nested children (equip via equipRef)
  - 'itself':  pass marker to resolve selection itself (not descendent targets);
    the behavior of this function with ancestors/groups is subject to change

name: rootMeanSquareErr
doc:
Fold a sample of numbers into their RMSE (root mean square error).
The RMSE function determines the RMSE between a sample set and
its mean using the n-degrees of freedom RMSE:

RMBE = sqrt( Σ(xᵢ - median)² ) / (n - nDegrees)

Examples:
samples.fold(rootMeanSquareErr)         // unbiased zero degrees of freedom
samples.fold(rootMeanSquareErr(_,_,1))  // 1 degree of freedom

name: uriHost
doc: Get the host Uri as a string or null

name: atan2
doc: Converts rectangular coordinates (x, y) to polar (r, theta).

name: isSpace
doc:
Is number is whitespace char: space \t \n \r \f

Examples:
isSpace("x".get(0))   >>  false
isSpace(" ".get(0))   >>  true
isSpace("\n".get(0))  >>  true

name: set

Set a collection item and return a new collection.
- List: set item by index key
- Dict: set item by key name

name: arcReadAllUnder
doc:
Read summary grid of arcs with an `arcOn` contained by the given parent.
For example given a site, this will return all arcs on the children
spaces, equip, and points via the siteRef.  This function only reads
from the local project.  The optional filter may be used to specify
additional filtering of the results.

Example:

  read(site).arcReadAllUnder

  // read open workorders under a given site

name: isNull
doc: Return if an object is null

name: occurred

Return if a timestamp is contained within a Date range.
Range may be any value supported by `toDateSpan`.  Timestamp

Examples:

ts.occurred(pastMonth())

name: uriFrag

name: recEdit
doc:
Record edit action.
NOTE: this function should only be used for actions.

name: matrixTranspose
doc: Transpose the given matrix which is any value accepted by `toMatrix`.

name: hisRollups
doc:
Perform a series of rollups over discrete intervals.  This
function works just like `hisRollup`, but takes a list of
folding functions.  Instead of a single value per input history,
there are a series of columns for each folding function.
For example if the input has a 'v0' and 'v1' column and the
folding functions are '[min, max]', then the resulting grid
will have 'v0_min', 'v0_max', 'v1_min', and 'v1_max'.

name: haystackPing

// reset multiple user's totp enrollment
userReadAll(<filter>).userMfaTotpReset
<pre

Disable tracing on the given connector.

name: specFitsExplain
doc:
Return a grid explaining why spec 'a' does not fit 'b'.
If 'a' does fit 'b' then return an empty grid.

name: fitsExplain
doc:
Return grid which explains how data fits the given spec.  This
function takes one or more recs and returns a grid.  For each rec
zero or more rows are returned with an error why the rec does not
fit the given type.  If a rec does fit the type, then zero rows are
returned for that record.

If you pass null for the spec, then each record is fit against
its declared 'spec' tag.  If a given rec is missing a 'spec' tag
then it is reported an error.

See `fits()` for list of options.

Example:
// validate tags on records only
readAll(vav and hotWaterHeating).fitsExplain(G36ReheatVav)

// validate tags and required points and other graph queries
readAll(vav and hotWaterHeating).fitsExplain(G36ReheatVav, {graph})

be running in a task context.  See `lib-task::doc#locals`.

name: sgEx
doc: Axon function

name: connPoints
doc:
Return the points for a connector as list of dicts.  The 'conn'
parameter may be anything accepted by `toRecId()`.  If 'conn' cannot be
be mapped to an active connector then raise an exception or return an
empty list based on checked flag.

Examples:
read(haystackConn).connPoints
connPoints(@my-conn-id)

name: validateFilter
doc: Raise an exception if string is not valid filter

name: userSetPassword
