name: stripUncommittable
doc:
Strip any tags which cannot be persistently committed to Folio.
This includes special tags such as 'hisSize' and any transient tags
the record has defined.  If 'val' is Dict, then a single Dict is returned.
Otherwise 'val' must be Dict[] or Grid and Dict[] is returned.
The 'mod' tag is stripped unless the '{mod}' option is specified.
The 'id' tag is not stripped for cases when adding records with
swizzled ids; pass '{-id}' in options to strip the 'id' tag also.

Examples:
// strip uncommittable tags and keep id
toCommit: rec.stripUncommittable

// strip uncommittable tags and the id tag
toCommit: rec.stripUncommittable({-id})

// strip uncommittable tags, but keep id and mod
toCommit: rec.stripUncommittable({mod})

name: refGen
doc: Generate a new unique Ref identifier

name: time
doc:
If val is a DateTime: get time portion of the timestamp.
If val is a Number: construct a time instance from hour, minutes,
secs (truncated to nearest second).

Examples:
now().time      // current time
time(20, 45)    // same as 20:45

name: second
doc: Get seconds of the time as integer between 0 to 59 from time or datetime

name: hisInWindows
doc:
Return true or false if the given timestamp is inside at least
one of the given windows.  The 'ts' parameter may be either a
DateTime or a Dict/Row with a 'ts' tag/column.  The 'windows'
parameter must be a grid of timestamp/duration rows. Windows
are start-inclusive and end-exclusive for timestamp comparisons.

See [Windows and Slices]`ext-hisKit::doc#windowsAndSlices`

Example:
  // filter out rows not inside a grid of windows
  hisRead(points, dates).findAll(row => hisInWindows(row, windows))

name: last
doc:
Get the last item from an ordered collection or return null
if the collection is empty:
- list: item at index -1
- grid: item at index -1
- stream: last item; see `docHaxall::Streams#last`

name: foldEnd
doc: The fold end marker value

name: any
doc:
Return if any the items in a list, dict, or grid match the
given test function.  If the collection is empty, then return
false.

If working with a list, the function takes '(val)'
or '(val, index)' and returns true or false.

If working with a dict, the function takes '(val)'
or '(val, name)' and returns true or false.

If working with a grid, the function takes '(row)'
or '(row, index)' and returns true or false.

If working with a string, the function takes '(char)'
or '(char, index)' and returns true or false.

If working with a stream, then function takes '(val)'
and returns true or false.  See `docHaxall::Streams#any`.

Examples:
[1, 3, 5].any v => v.isOdd  >>  true
[2, 4, 6].any(isOdd)        >>  false

name: bitXor
doc: Bitwise xor: 'a ^ b'

name: gridReplace
doc:
Replace every grid cell with the given 'from' value with the 'to' value.
The resulting grid has the same grid and col meta.  Replacement comparison
is by via equality via '==' operator, so it will only replace
scalar values or null.

Example:
grid.gridReplace(null, 0)   // replace all null cells with zero
grid.gridReplace(na(), 0)   // replace all NA cells with zero

name: userBackups
doc: List all the user database backups

name: isOdd
doc: Return if an integer is an odd number.

name: ioFromBase64
doc:
Return an I/O handle to decode from a base64 string.
Also see `ioToBase64()` and `sys::Buf.fromBase64`

Example:
// decode base64 to a string
ioFromBase64("c2t5c3Bhcms").ioReadStr

name: ruleSparksCount
doc:
Return number of sparks as queried by `ruleSparks()`.
The parameters have the same semantics, except this function is
more efficient since it does not build a result grid first.

name: specs
doc:
List Xeto specs as a list of their [dict]`xeto::Spec` representation.
Scope may one of the following:
- null: return all the top-level specs currently in the using scope
- lib: return all the top-level specs declared in given library
- list of lib: all specs in given libraries

A filter may be specified to filter the specs found in the scope.  The
dict representation for filtering supports a "slots" tag on each spec
with a Dict of the effective slots name.  This allows filtering slots
using the syntax 'slots->someName'.

NOTE: returns the same Spec instances as returned by `xeto::LibNamespace`.
The spec meta is modeled using full fidelity and *not* haystack fidelity.
Use `specMeta` to normalize to haystack fidelity for use inside Axon.

Examples:
specs()                  // specs in using scope
specLib("ph").specs      // specs in a given library
specs(null, abstract)    // filter specs with filter expression
specs(null, slots->ahu)  // filter specs have ahu tag

name: taskSendLater
doc:
Schedule a message for delivery after the specified period of
duration has elapsed.  Once the period has elapsed the message is
appended to the end of the task's queue.  Return a future to
track the asynchronous result.  See `lib-task::doc#messaging`.

name: matrixMult
doc:
Multiply two matrices and return new matrix.  The parameters may
be any value supported `toMatrix`.  Matrix 'a' column count must match
matrix 'b' row count.

name: trap
doc:
Function for the '->' operator.  If the given value is a dict,
then get a value by name, or throw UnknownNameErr if name not mapped.
If the value is a Ref, then perform a checked 'readById', then perform
the name lookup.

The trap function maybe be accessed using the '->' shortcut operator:
   dict->foo  >>>  dict.trap("foo")

See `docHaxall::AxonLang#getAndTrap`.

name: readByIdPersistentTags
doc:
Given record id, read only the persistent tags from Folio.
Also see `readByIdTransientTags` and `readById`.

name: ruleReobserve
doc:
Clear today's observed flag to re-observe sparks.  Targets and rules
may be null for wildcard or any value accepted by `toRecList`.  This
function only clears the observed flag.  New observations will not be
fired until the rule is re-run by hot period or ruleRecompute.
Note: this function is subject to change.

name: parseDiscreteBoolPeriods
doc:
Parse a Base64 encoded timeline of discrete Bool periods
as encoded by `discreteBoolPeriods`.  Return a grid of minutely
offsets and Bool/NA values with the columns 'offset'
and 'val'. If val is null, then return null.

name: instantiate
doc:
Create the default instance for a given spec.
Raise exception if spec is abstract.

The default behavior for dict types is to return a single Dict.
However, if the type has a constrainted query, then an entire graph
can be instantiated via the '{graph}' option in which case a Dict[] is
returned.  In graph mode an 'id' is generated for recs for cross-linking.

Also see `xeto::LibNamespace.instantiate`.

NOTE: this function forces the 'haystack' option to force all
non-Haystack scalars to be simple strings.

Options:
- 'graph': marker tag to instantiate a graph of recs
- 'abstract': marker to supress error if spec is abstract

Examples:
// evaluates to 2000-01-01
instantiate(Date)

// evaluates to dict {equip, vav, hotWaterHeating, ...}
instantiate(G36ReheatVav)

// evaluates to dict[] of vav + points from constrained query
instantiate(G36ReheatVav, {graph})

name: refProjName
doc:
Given an absolute ref, return its project name.  If the ref
is not formatted as "p:proj:r:xxx", then raise an exception or
return null based on the checked flag:

Examples:
refProjName(@p:demo:r:xxx)   >>  "demo"
refProjName(@r:xxx)          >>  raises exception
refProjName(@r:xxx, false)   >>  null

name: specBase
doc:
Base spec from which the given spec directly inherits.
Returns null if spec is 'sys::Obj' itself.

Examples:
specBase(Str)    >>  sys::Scalar
specBase(Meter)  >>  ph::Equip
specType(Equip)  >>  ph::Entity

name: unitsEq
doc:
Return if the two numbers have the same unit.  If either
of the numbers if null return false.

name: quantile
doc:
Computes the p*th* quantile of a list of numbers, according to the specified interpolation method.
The value p must be a number between 0.0 to 1.0.

- **linear** (default): Interpolates proportionally between the two closest values
- **nearest**: Rounds to the nearest data point
- **lower**: Rounds to the nearest lower data point
- **higher**: Rounds to the nearest higher data point
- **midpoint**: Averages two nearest values

Usage:
[1,2,3].fold(quantile(p, method))

Examples:
[10,10,10,25,100].fold(quantile(0.7 )) => 22 //default to linear
[10,10,10,25,100].fold(quantile(0.7, "nearest")) => 25
[10,10,10,25,100].fold(quantile(0.7, "lower")) => 10
[10,10,10,25,100].fold(quantile(0.7, "higher")) => 25
[10,10,10,25,100].fold(quantile(0.7, "linear")) => 22 //same as no arg
[10,10,10,25,100].fold(quantile(0.7, "midpoint")) => 17.5

Detailed Logic:
p: percentile (decimal 0-1)
n: list size
rank: p * (n-1) // this is the index of the percentile in your list
// if rank is an integer, return list[rank]
// if rank is not an integer, interpolate via one of the above methods (illustrated below in examples)

[1,2,3,4,5].percentile(0.5) => 3 // rank=2 is an int so we can index[2] directly

[10,10,10, 25, 100].percentile(0.7, method)
rank = (0.7 * 4) => 2.8

//adjust rank based on method
nearest =  index[3]                // => 25
lower =    index[2]                // => 10
higher =   index[3]                // => 25

//or interpolate for these methods

//takes the 2 closest indices and calculates midpoint
midpoint = (25-10)/2 + 10          // => 17.5

//takes the 2 closest indices and calculates weighted average
linear =   (0.2 * 10) + (0.8 * 25) // => 22

name: ioReadCsv
doc:
Read a CSV (comma separated values) file into memory as a Grid.
CSV format is implemented as specified by RFC 4180:
- rows are delimited by a newline
- cells are separated by 'delimiter' char
- cells containing the delimiter, '"' double quote, or
newline are quoted; quotes are escaped as '""'
- empty cells are normalized into null

The following options are supported:
- delimiter: separator char as string, default is ","
- noHeader: if present then don't treat first row as col names,
instead use "v0", "v1", etc

Also see `ioStreamCsv`, `ioEachCsv`, `ioWriteCsv`, and `docHaystack::Csv`.

name: mean
doc:
Fold a sample of numbers into their standard average or arithmetic
mean.  This function is the same as [core::avg]`avg()`.  Nulls
values are ignored.  Return null if no values.

Example:
[2, 4, 5, 3].fold(mean)

name: provNewImage
doc:
Create a [provImage]`ext-prov::doc#provImage` from an *ordered* list of manifest recs.
The image will have the given display name, and you can specify additional tags to
apply to the image.

  provNewImage([@build-3-0-22, @patch-3-0-22, @myOverlay], "Custom 3.0.22", {version:"3.0.22", foo})

name: hisPeriodUnion
doc:
Find the union of two or more historical periods. The 'periods'
parameter may be:
  - Grid?: a single grid where the first column is a "ts" column,
  and all remaining columns are named anything and contain period values.
  - Grid?[]: a list of grids where each grid has the format described above,

All input grids must be [normalized]`hisPeriodNormalize()`

The resulting grid has "ts" and "v0" column, where "v0" is duration in hours.

Example:
  // find periods when either a, b, or c is on
  hisPeriodUnion([a, b, c])

name: markdownInlineFormatter
doc: Axon function

name: today
doc: Return today's Date according to context's time zone

name: replicaBackup
doc:
Asynchronously kick off a new local backup for given replica.  Requires
admin permission.  Return a future to track completion of the backup.
The future's result value is subject to change.
See `docSkySpark::Replication#backups`.

Examples:
  replicaBackup("myProj")

name: recNew
doc:
Record create action.
NOTE: this function should only be used for actions.

name: xqTrace
doc: Execute the XQuery and return debug tracing

name: toRecIdList
doc:
Coerce a value to a list of Ref identifiers:
- Ref returns itself as list of one
- Ref[] returns itself
- Dict return 'id' tag
- Dict[] return 'id' tags
- Grid return 'id' column

name: ioReadXeto
doc:
Read a Xeto data file into memory as a Haystack data type.
See `xeto::LibNamespace.compileData` for details and options.

name: avg
doc:
Fold multiple values into their standard average or arithmetic
mean.  This function is the same as [math::mean]`mean`.  Null
values are ignored.  Return null if no values.

Example:
[7, 2, 3].fold(avg)  >>  4

name: gridRowsToDict
doc:
Convert grid rows into a dict of name/val pairs.  The name/value
pairs are derived from each row using the given functions.  The
functions take '(row, index)'

Example:
// create dict of sites with dis:area pairs
readAll(site).gridRowsToDict(s=>s.dis.toTagName, s=>s->area)

name: commit
doc:
Commit one or more diffs to the folio database.
The argument may be one of the following:
- result of `diff()`
- list of `diff()` to commit multiple diffs at once
- stream of `diff()`; see `docHaxall::Streams#commit`.

If one diff is passed, return the new record.  If a list
of diffs is passed return a list of new records.

This is a synchronous blocking call which will return
the new record or records as the result.

Examples:
// add new record
newRec: commit(diff(null, {dis:"New Rec!"}, {add}))

// add someTag to some group of records
readAll(filter).toRecList.map(r => diff(r, {someTag})).commit

name: renameCols
doc:
Return a new grid with multiple columns renamed.
Mapping must be a dict of old to new names.  Old column names
not found are ignored.

Example:
readAll(site).renameCols({dis:"title", geoAddr:"subtitle"})

name: isTagName
doc:
Return if the given string is legal tag name -
see `haystack::Etc.isTagName`

name: hisSlidingWindows
doc:
Compute of grid of timestamp/duration windows.  The window start
times are defined by 'range' and 'step' parameter.  The window
duration is defined by 'dur' parameter.  The last window's end
timestamp is always before or equal to the range ending timestamp.

 - 'range': may be any date/datetime range supported by `hisRead` or
   may be a history grid itself in which case the hisStart/hisEnd
   grid meta tags are used as the range
 - 'dur': Number with duration unit, standard unit is hour
 - 'step': Number with duration unit, standard unit is hour

See [Windows and Slices]`ext-hisKit::doc#windowsAndSlices`

Example:

  // create sliding 1hr windows every 30min
  hisSlidingWindows(2011-07-04, 1hr, 30min)

  // resulting grid
  ts, v0
  4-Jul-2011 00:00:00, 1hr
  4-Jul-2011 00:30:00, 1hr
  4-Jul-2011 01:00:00, 1hr
  4-Jul-2011 01:30:00, 1hr
  ...

name: toSpan
doc:
Convert the following objects into a `haystack::Span`:
- 'Span': return itself
- 'Span+tz': update timezone using same dates only if aligned to midnight
- 'Str': return `haystack::Span.fromStr` using current timezone
- 'Str+tz': return `haystack::Span.fromStr` using given timezone
- 'DateTime..DateTime': range of two DateTimes
- 'Date..DateTime': start day for date until the end timestamp
- 'DateTime..Date': start timestamp to end of day for end date
- 'DateTime': span of a single timestamp
- 'DateSpan': anything accepted by `toDateSpan` in current timezone
- 'DateSpan+tz': anything accepted by `toDateSpan` using given timezone

name: queryNamed
trim
doc:
Trim whitespace from the beginning and end of the string.  For the purposes
of this function, whitespace is defined as any character equal to or less
than the 0x20 space character (including ' ', '\r', '\n', and '\t').

Examples:
" abc ".trim   >>  "abc"
"abc".trim     >>  "abc"

name: bitShiftr
doc: Bitwise right shift: 'a >> b'

name: toMatrix
doc:
Convert a general grid to an optimized matrix grid.  Matrixs are two
dimensional grids of Numbers.  Columns are named "v0", "v1", "v2", etc.
Grid meta is preserved, but not column meta.  Numbers in the resulting
matrix are unitless; any units passed in are stripped.

The following options are supported:
- nullVal (Number): replace null values in the grid with this value
- naVal (Number): replace NA values in the grid with this value

pre>
toMatrix(grid, {nullVal: 0, naVal: 0})
<pre

To create a sparse or initialized matrix you can pass a Dict with the
the following tags (all required)
toMatrix({rows:10, cols: 1000, init: 0})

name: taskCur
doc:
Return current task if running within the context of an asynchronous
task.  If context is not within a task, then return null or raise
an exception based on checked flag.

name: arcEdit
doc:
Commit a diff to an existing arc rec.  Orig must be an arc record.  If
part is non-null, then it is a dict with new part to add the arc. Return
new arc document as grid.  See `ext-arc::doc#axon`.

name: bitShiftl
doc: Bitwise left shift: 'a << b'

name: isFunc
doc: Return if an object is a function type

name: tags
doc: List tag definitions in the context namespace as Def[].

name: sortr
doc:
Reverse sort a list or grid.  This function works just
like `sort` except sorts in reverse.

name: isNonNull
doc: Return if an object is not null

name: asin
doc: Return the arc sine.

name: tanh
doc: Return hyperbolic tangent.

name: userCommit
doc:
Commit one or more diffs to the user database.
The argument may be one of the following:
  - result of `diff()`
  - list of `diff()` to commit multiple diffs at once

If one diff is passed, return the new record. If a list
of diffs is passed, return a list of new records.

When creating a new user the following tags must be specified:
 - `id` - Must be '@<username>'
 - `user`
 - `username` - Must match the 'id'.
 - `tz`
 - `userRole`

If the `userAuth` tag is not specified, a "dummy" SCRAM configuration will be
be generated, but the user will **not** be able to log in until a password
is set. This can be done in Axon using `userSetPassword()`. Otherwise, you
will need to reset their password in the user manager, or if email reset is
enabled the user can do it from the login page using the forgot my password feature.

Examples:
pre>
// add a new user
userTags: {id:@bach, user, username:"bach", userRole:"op", tz:"New_York"}
newRec: userCommit(diff(null, userTags, {add}))

// update the appAccess tag on all operator users
userReadAll(user and userRole=="op")
  .toRecList
  .map(r => diff(r, {appAccess:"energy,his"}))
  .userCommit

// remove all disabled users
userReadAll(user and disabled)
  .toRecList
  .map(r => diff(r, null, {remove}))
  .userCommit
<pre

name: startsWith
doc:
Return if Str starts with the specified Str.

Examples:
"hi there".startsWith("hi")   >>  true
"hi there".startsWith("foo")  >>  false

name: replicaBackupCleanup
doc:
Delete all local replica backups older than the given duration or date.
Requires admin permission.  See `docSkySpark::Replication#backups`.

Examples:
  replicaBackupCleanup("myProj", 15day)       // delete all backups older than 15 days
  replicaBackupCleanup("myProj", 2017-11-15)  // delete all backups before given date

name: sysMods
doc:
List the installed system mods.
Result format is subject to change

name: pastMonth
doc: DateSpan for last 30days 'today-30days..today'

name: logInfo
doc: Log at the "info" level - see `log`.

name: haystackSyncCur
doc: Deprecated - use `connSyncCur()`

name: thisMonth
doc: DateSpan for this month as '1st..28-31'

name: isNumber
doc: Return if an object is a number type

name: equals
doc:
Return if two values are equivalent.  Unlike the standard '==' operator
this function will compare the contents of collection values such
as lists, dicts, and grids.  For non-collection values, the result
is the same as the '==' operator.  This function does not work with
lazy grids such as hisRead result.

name: uriDecode
doc:
Parse an ASCII percent encoded string into a Uri according to RFC 3986.
All %HH escape sequences are translated into octects, and then the octect
sequence is UTF-8 decoded into a Str. The '+' character in the query section
is unescaped into a space. If checked if true then throw ParseErr if the
string is a malformed URI or if not encoded correctly, otherwise return
null.  Use `parseUri` to parse from standard form.  See `sys::Uri` for
a detailed discussion on standard and encoded forms.

Examples:
"foo bar".parseUri     >>  `foo bar`
"foo%20bar".uriDecode  >>  `foo bar`

name: setColMeta
doc:
Return a new grid with column meta-data replaced by given meta dict.
If column not found, then return given grid.
Also see `addColMeta` and `docHaxall::Streams#setColMeta`.

name: energyBaselinePrevYear
doc:
Generate a baseline his grid based on last year's data.
Data from exactly 364days ago is used to ensure alignment by weekday.

name: parseDiscreteEnumPeriods
doc:
Parse a base64 encoded string timeline of discrete enum periods
as encoded by `discreteEnumPeriods`.  Return a grid of minutely
offsets and enum ordinal values with the columns 'offset'
and 'ordinal'.  If the rec is passed with the 'enum' tag, then
an additional 'name' column specifies the ordinal to name mapping.
If val is null, then return null.

name: marker
doc: Get the marker value singleton `haystack::Marker.val`

name: readAllStream
doc:
Reall all records which match filter as stream of Dict records.
See `docHaxall::Streams#readAllStream`.

name: toStr
doc: Convert an obj to its string representation

name: transpose
doc:
Perform a matrix transpose on the grid.  The cells of the
first column because the display names for the new columns.
Columns 1..n become the new rows.

Example:
readAll(site).transpose

name: ioEachLine
doc:
For each line of the given source stream call the given function
with two parameters: Str line and zero based Number line number.
Lines are processed according to `sys::InStream.eachLine`.

name: watchRemove
doc: Remove a grid of recs from an existing watch and return grid passed in.

name: lastYear
doc: DateSpan for year previous to this year 'Jan-1..Dec-31'

name: parseNumber
doc:
Parse a Str into a number with an option unit.  If invalid
format and checked is false return null, otherwise throw ParseErr.
Also see `parseInt` and `parseFloat` to parse basic integers and
floating point numbers without a unit.

Examples:
parseNumber("123")
parseNumber("123kW")
parseNumber("123.567").format("#.000")

name: ioEachCsv
doc:
Iterate the rows of a CSV file (comma separated values) and callback
the given function with two parameters: Str[] cells of current row
and zero based Number line number.

The following options are supported:
- delimiter: separator char as string, default is ","

Also `ioReadCsv`, `ioWriteCsv`, and `docHaystack::Csv`.

name: readById
doc:
Read a record from database by 'id'.  If not found
throw UnknownRecErr or return null based on checked flag.
In Haxall all refs are relative, but in SkySpark refs may
be prefixed with something like "p:projName:r:".  This function
will accept both relative and absolute refs.

Examples:
readById(@2b00f9dc-82690ed6)          // relative ref literal
readById(@:demo:r:2b00f9dc-82690ed6)  // project absolute literal
readById(id)                          // read using variable
readById(equip->siteRef)              // read from ref tag

name: isKeyword
doc: Return if given string is an Axon keyword

name: parseUri
doc:
Parse a string into a Uri instance.  If the string cannot be parsed
into a valid Uri and checked is false then return null, otherwise
throw ParseErr.  This function converts an URI from *standard form*.
Use `uriDecode` to convert a string from *escaped form*.  See `sys::Uri`
for a detailed discussion on standard and escaped forms.

Examples:
"foo bar".parseUri     >>  `foo bar`
"foo%20bar".uriDecode  >>  `foo bar`

name: taskSendWhenComplete
doc:
Schedule a message for delivery after the given future has completed.
Completion may be due to the future returning a result, throwing an
exception, or cancellation.  Return a future to track the asynchronous
result.  See `lib-task::doc#messaging`.

name: moveTo
doc:
Find the given item in a list, and move it to the given index.  All
the other items are shifted accordingly.  Negative indexes may
used to access an index from the end of the list.  If the item is
not found then this is a no op.  Return new list.

Examples:
[10, 11, 12].moveTo(11, 0)  >>  [11, 10, 12]
[10, 11, 12].moveTo(11, -1) >>  [10, 12, 11]

name: matrixFitLinearRegression
doc:
Given a matrix of y coordinates and a matrix of multiple x coordinates
compute the best fit multiple linear regression equation using
the ordinary least squares method.  Both 'y' and 'x' may be any value
accepted by `toMatrix`.

The resulting linear equation for r X coordinates is:

yᵢ = bias + b₁xᵢ₁ + b₂xᵢ₂ +...+ bᵣxᵢᵣ

The equation is returned as a grid.  The grid meta:
- 'bias': bias or zero coefficient which is independent of any of the x factors
- 'r2':  R² coefficient of determination as a number between 1.0 (perfect correlation) and 0.0 (no correlation)
- 'r': the square root of R², referred to as the correlation coefficient
- 'rowCount': the number of rows of data used in the correlation
For each X factor there is a row with the following tags:
- 'b': the correlation coefficient for the given X factor

name: deprecatedTags
doc:
Return tags which have been deprecated as grid with following columns:
  - old: original name of tag deprecated
  - new: new tag name which should be used or Remove.val
  - version: SkySpark version when tag was tag was deprecated
  - comment: free form comments about the deprecation

This function can be paired with `renameTags()` to script migration.

name: weekOfYear
doc:
Given a DateTime or Date, return the week number of the year.  The
result is a number between 1 and 53 using the given start of week weekday
as number 0-6 (defaults start of week for current locale).

name: uriName
doc: Get the name Str of a Uri (last item in path).

name: hisFindTroughs
doc:
Filter a history grid so that only numeric samples that represent
a trough are returned.  A trough is defined as a timestamp/value
pair where both the previous and next value is larger.  Non-numeric
columns are implicitly filtered out.  If the source grid has multiple
numeric value columns, then non-trough cells are set to null.
Also see `hisFindPeaks`.

name: iconUri
doc:
Create a virtual file URI to an [icon]`docFresco::Icons`.  If
name is null, then default to the "unknown" icon.
  iconUri("lock")
  iconUri("lock", "outline")
  iconUri("lock", "outline", "blue")

name: ioWritePdf
doc:
Render data to a PDF file.  The grid meta "view" tag determines
the visualization:
- 'table': render grid as [table]`docFresco::Tables` (default)
- 'chart': render grid as [chart]`docFresco::Charts`
- 'fandoc': render string as [fandoc]`fandoc::pod-doc#overview`
- 'text': render as plaintext

Options:
- `pageSize`: determines the PDF page size

Examples:
// render as chart with default page size
read(power).hisRead(yesterday).ioWritePdf(`io/portrait.pdf`)

// render as chart with landscape page size of 11" x 8.5"
read(power).hisRead(yesterday).ioWritePdf(`io/landscape.pdf`, {pageSize:"11in,8.5in"})

// render table as single auto-fit page
readAll(site).ioWritePdf(`io/sites.pdf`, {pageSize:"auto"})

Note: this feature is available in SkySpark only

name: pointThermistorTables
doc: Return grid of thermistor table names as grid with one 'name' column

name: cols
doc:
Get the columns from a grid as a list.

Example:
// get name of first column
readAll(site).cols.first.name

name: elecKwToKwhHis
doc:
Function to create a [computed history]`ext-his::doc#computed`
for `energy` kWh electrical consumption from `demand` "kW" electrical demand.
This function creates an average hourly rollup from the kW data, but
in order to work correctly the kW samples must be uniformly sampled
across the hour (such as every 15min), and the point should have `hisMode`
set to 'consumption'.

name: xqDefine
doc:
Define a variable available to subsequent Axon expressions.  This
provides an easy way to marshall data across the cluster without
encoding data to an Axon expression.  See `skyarcd::XQuery.define`.

name: equipToPoints
doc:
Given a 'equip' record Dict, return a grid of its points.
If this function is overridden you MUST NOT use an XQuery to
resolve points; this function must return local only points.

name: gridColKinds
doc:
Given a grid return the types used in each column as a grid
with the following:
- 'name': string name of the column
- 'kind': all the different value kinds in the column separated by "|"
- 'count': total number of rows with column with a non-null value
Also see `readAllTagNames`.

Example:
readAll(site).gridColKinds

name: licStatus
doc:
Return current licensing status as Dict with following tags:
  - dis: summary of licensing status
  - mode: status string
  - usage: current point usage across projects
  - points: licensing capacity
  - level: info, warn, or err
  - devOnly: marker if development only license

name: coordDist
doc:
Compute the great-circle distance between two Coords.
The result is a distance in meters using the haversine forumula.

name: libAdd
doc:
Enable a library by name in the runtime:
libAdd("mqtt")

name: uiLink
doc: Host relative URI to hyperlink to a Fresco view - see `docFresco::Export#links`

name: arcbeamReadAll
doc:
Read a grid of records from remote node with a filter.

Supported options for all remote node types:

Supported [Niagara Options]`docAppNotes::NiagaraArcbeam#niagaraOpts`

Examples:
  arcbeamReadAll(@conn, ahu and equip)
  arcbeamReadAll(@conn, ahu and equip, {limit:10})

name: cos
doc: Return the cosine of angle in radians.

name: libs
doc: List the lib definitions in the context namespace as Def[].

name: col
doc:
Get a column by its name.  If not resolved then
return null or throw UnknownNameErr based on checked flag.

Example:
// get meta data for given columm
read(temp).hisRead(today).col("ts").meta

name: fitLinearRegression
doc:
Given a grid of x, y coordinates compute the best fit linear
regression equation using the ordinary least squares method.
The first column of the grid is used for 'x' and the second
column is 'y'.  Any rows without a Number for both x and y
are skipped.  Any special Numbers (infinity/NaN) are skipped.

Options:
- 'x': column name to use for x if not first column
- 'y': column name to use for y if not second column

The resulting linear equation is:

yᵢ = mxᵢ + b

The equation is returned as a dictionary with these keys:
- 'm': slope of the best fit regression line
- 'b': intercept of the best fit regression line
- 'r2':  R² coefficient of determination as a number between
1.0 (perfect correlation) and 0.0 (no correlation)
- 'xmin': minimum value of x variable in sample data
- 'xmax': maximum value of x variable in sample data
- 'ymin': minimum value of y variable in sample data
- 'ymax': maximum value of y variable in sample data

Also see `matrixFitLinearRegression` to compute a multiple linear
regression.

Example:
data: [{x:1, y:2},
{x:2, y:4},
{x:4, y:4},
{x:6, y:5}].toGrid
fitLinearRegression(data)

>>> {m:0.4915, b: 2.1525, r2: 0.7502}

name: toSpaces
doc:
Map a set of recs to to a grid of [spaces]`space`.  The 'recs'
parameter may be any value accepted by `toRecList()`.  Return empty
grid if no mapping is found.  The following mappings are supported:
- recs with 'space' tag are mapped as themselves
- recs with 'spaceRef' tag are mapped to their parent space
- recs with 'site' are mapped to spaces with parent 'siteRef'

Examples:
read(site).toSpaces      // return children spaces within site
read(equip).toSpaces     // return equip's parent space
read(point).toSpaces     // return point's parent space
read(space).toSpaces     // return space itself

name: readByIdTransientTags
doc:
Given record id, read only the transient tags from Folio.
Also see `readByIdPersistentTags` and `readById`.

name: isLower
doc:
Is number an ASCII lowercase alphabetic char: a-z

Examples:
isUpper("a".get(0))  >>  true
isUpper("A".get(0))  >>  false
isUpper("5".get(0))  >>  false

name: connTraceIsEnabled
doc:
Return true or false if given connector has its tracing enabled.
The 'conn' parameter can be anything acceptable by `toRecId()`.

name: pointSetDef
doc:
Set the relinquish default value (level-17) of writable point.
See `pointWrite`.

name: parseFilter
doc:
Parse a filter string into a [Filter]`haystack::Filter` instance.
The resulting filter can then be used with `read()`, `readAll()`,
`filter()`, or `filterToFunc()`.

Example:
str: "point and kw"
readAll(parseFilter(str))

name: matchPointVal
doc:
Return if a point value matches a given critera:
- match any values which are equal via '==' operator
- zero matches false (0% ==> false)
- non-zero matches true (not 0% ==> true)
- numerics can be matches with range
- match can be a function which takes the value

Examples:
matchPointVal(false, false)     >>  true
matchPointVal(0, false)         >>  true
matchPointVal(33, false)        >>  false
matchPointVal(33, true)         >>  true
matchPointVal(33, 0..40)        >>  true
matchPointVal(90, 0..40)        >>  false
matchPointVal(4) x => x.isEven  >>  true

name: ioWriteXml
doc: Write a grid to an [XML]`docHaxall::Xml` file.

name: pointWriteArray
doc:
Return the current priority array state of a writable point.
The point may be any value accepted by `toRec`.  The result is
returned grid with following columns:
- level: number from 1 - 17 (17 is default)
- levelDis: human description of level
- val: current value at level or null
- who: who last controlled the value at this level

name: arcbeamReadById
doc:
Read a record from the remote node by its unique id and return as dict.
If the record is not found, then return null or raise UnknownRecErr
based on checked flag.

Examples:
  arcbeamReadById(@conn, @xyx-123)

name: folioBackup
doc:
Asynchronously kick off a new backup for current project.
Requires admin permission.  See `skyarcd::Proj.backup`
To run a snapshot a background job use `jobFolioBackup`.
Return a future to track completion of the backup.  The
future's result value is subject to change.

name: recDup
doc:
Record duplicate action.
This function implicitly operates on all contained recs.
NOTE: this function should only be used for actions.

name: replace
doc:
String replace of all occurrences of 'from' with 'to'.
All three parameters must be strings.

Examples:
"hello".replace("hell", "t")  >>  "to"
"aababa".replace("ab", "-")   >>  "a--a"

name: recForm
doc:
Return a data grid for a form view to edit the specified record.
If the record is null a new default one is created from the template.
See `recFormCommit` for the submit action function.
TODO: subject to change

name: foldCols
doc:
Fold a set of columns in each row into a new folded column
and return a new grid.  The columns to fold are selected by the
'colSelector' function and removed from the result.  The selector
may be a list of string names or a function which takes a Col
and returns true to select it.  The folding function uses same
semantics as `fold`.


Example:
// consider grid 'g' with the following structure:
a    b    c
---  ---  ---
1    10   100
2    20   200

// foldCols, add b and c together to create new bc column
g.foldCols(["b", "c"], "bc", sum)

// yields this grid:
a    bc
---  ---
1    110
2    220

// we could also replace list of col names with a function
colSel: col => col.name == "b" or col.name == "c"
g.foldCols(colSel, "bc", sum)

name: taskRestart
doc:
Restart a task.  This kills the tasks and discards any
pending messages in its queue.  See `lib-task::doc#lifecycle`.

name: pointConvert
doc:
Evaluate a [point conversion]`ext-point::doc#convert`. First
parameter is point to test (anything accepted by `toRec`) or null
to use empty dict.

Examples:
pointConvert(null, "+ 2 * 10", 3)
pointConvert(null, "hexToNumber()", "ff")
pointConvert(null, "°C => °F", 20°C)

name: recDisable
doc:
Record disable action.
NOTE: this function should only be used for actions.

name: lint
doc:
Run the lint report on the given recs.  Recs may be any value
accepted by `toRecList` or if null then lint is run on the
entire project. By default lint will only run for 10sec or
until it finds 1000 issues.  You can adjust these limts via
the opts parameter:
  - timeout: max amount of time to run lint (10sec default)
  - limit: max number of issues (1000 default)

Pass '{-timeout, -limit}' to remove the timeout and limit.

Results are returned as grid with the following columns:
  - id: record id
  - tag: tag name with the lint issue
  - logLevel: either "warn" or "err"
  - msg: issue as string

name: sum
doc:
Fold multiple values into their numeric sum.
Return null if no values.

name: flatten
doc:
Flatten a list to a single level.  If given a list of
grids, then flatten rows to a single grid.  Also see
`sys::List.flatten` and `haystack::Etc.gridFlatten`.

Examples:
// flatten a list of numbers
[1, [2, 3], [4, [5, 6]]].flatten  >>  [1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]

// flatten a list of grids
["Carytown", "Gaithersburg"].map(n=>readAll(siteRef->dis==n)).flatten

name: ruleSparkExplain
doc:
Return explanation information for a spark.  The param must be a
spark dict - typically a row from the `ruleSparks()` function.

The explanation result is a dict with following tags:
 - spark: the spark dict parameter
 - explain: fandoc string taken from 'explain' tag on the spark if available
 - help: the fandoc string for rule help tag
 - points: Ref[] for points used as inputs for the spark
 - tuning: Dict of tuning parameters

If the spark itself defines a 'points' tag with a Ref[] value, then it
is used.  Otherwise, the system will attempt to resolve point inputs
from the rule's component input bindings.

If the spark defines a 'tuning' tag, then it is used.  Otherwise, the
system will include the current tuning parameters.  Note that it is
possible that tuning tags have been modified since the spark was computed.
So the tuning Dict included by this function may not reflect the tuning
actually used.

This function is used by the Explain action in the Spark|Equip view.
Override this function to provide customized explain information.  Each
tag in the explain dict is displayed in the UI as follows:
  - dicts are shown using an info icon popup
  - multi-line strings are shown as fandoc
  - all other values are shown using their string value

Example:
  spark: ruleSparks(null, lastWeek, null).first
  ruleSparkExplain(spark)

name: toWeather
doc:
Attempt to get the weather station record from:
  - if rec has `weatherStation` tag, then return it
  - if rec has `weatherStationRef` tag, then dereference
  - if rec has `siteRef`, attempt to dereference from that

name: xetoReload
doc: Reload all the Xeto libraries

name: connTraceClear
doc:
Clear the trace log for the given connector.
The 'conn' parameter can be anything acceptable by `toRecId()`.

name: floor
doc:
Return the largest whole number less than or equal to val.
Result has same unit as 'val'.

name: date
doc:
If val is a DateTime: get date portion of the timestamp.
If val is a Number: construct a date instance from year, month, day

Examples:
now().date         // same as today()
date(2010, 12, 1)  // same as 2010-12-01

name: cosh
doc: Return the hyperbolic cosine.

name: uriExt
doc: Get the URI extension of a Uri as a string or null.

name: taskLocalRemove
doc:
Remove a task local variable by name. Must be running in a task
context.  See `lib-task::doc#locals`.

name: dictToHttpJson
dateTime
doc:
Construct a DateTime from a date, time, and timezone name.
If timezone is null, use system default.

name: removeCols
doc:
Return a new grid with all the given columns removed.
Columns can be Str names or Col instances.
Also see `docHaxall::Streams#removeCols`.

name: parseSymbol
doc:
Parse a Str into a Symbol.  If the string is not a valid Symbol
identifier then raise ParseErr or return null based on checked flag.
The string must *not* include a leading "^".

Examples:
parseSymbol("func:now")

name: extSettings
doc: Return ext settings form data - see `docSkySpark::Exts#settings`

name: findAll
doc:
Find all the items in a list, dict, or grid by applying
the given filter function.  Also see `find`.

If working with a list, the filter should be a function
that takes '(val)' or '(val, index)'.  It should return
true to keep the item.

If working with a dict, the filter should be a function
that takes '(val)' or '(val, name)'.  It should return
the true to keep the name/value pair.

If working with a grid, the filter function takes '(row)'
or '(row, index)' and returns true to keep the row.  The
resulting grid shares the original's grid meta and columns.

If working with a stream, the filter takes '(val)' and returns
true to match.  See `docHaxall::Streams#findAll`.

Examples:
// find all the strings longer than 3 chars
["ape", "bat", "charlie", "dingo"].findAll(x => x.size > 3)

// find even numbers
[0, 1, 2, 3, 4].findAll(isEven)

// find all the sites greater than 10,000ft from grid
readAll(site).findAll(s => s->area > 10_000ft²)

name: uriPort
doc: Get the port of a Uri as a Number or null

name: hostMeta
doc: Read host metadata dict

name: about
doc: Return [about]`op:about` dict

name: ioReadLines
doc:
Read an I/O handle into memory as a list of string lines.
Lines are processed according to `sys::InStream.readLine` semanatics.

By default the maximum line size read is 4kb of Unicode
characters (not bytes).  This limit may be overridden using
the option key "limit".

Examples:
ioReadLines(`io/file.txt`)
ioReadLines(`io/file.txt`, {limit: 10_000})

name: trimEnd
doc:
Trim whitespace only from the end of the string.
See `trim` for definition of whitespace.

Examples:
" abc ".trimEnd  >>  " abc"
"abc".trimEnd    >>  "abc"

name: rowToList
doc:
Get a grid row as a list of cells.  Sparse cells are included as null.
Also see `colToList()`.

Example:
readAll(equip).first.rowToList

name: futureWaitFor
doc:
Block until a future transitions to a completed state (ok,
err, or canceled).  If timeout is null then block forever,
otherwise raise a TimeoutErr if timeout elapses.  Return future.
See `lib-task::doc#futures`.

name: xqExecute
doc: Execute the XQuery and return results

name: xqPivot
doc:
Transform previous step into pivot table - see `skyarcd::XQuery.pivot`
Note: column names and meta are subject to change.

name: connClose
doc:
Force the given connector closed and return a future.
The future result is the connector rec dict.  The 'conn'
parameter may be anything accepted by `toRecId()`.

Examples:
read(conn).connClose
connClose(connId)

name: month
doc: Get month as integer between 1 to 12 from date or datetime

name: ioCrc
doc:
Generate a cycle reduancy check code as a Number.
See `sys::Buf.crc` for available algorithms.

Example:
ioCrc("foo", "CRC-32").toHex

name: installStatus
doc: Return install status as Dict with following tags: done, percent, msg, err, errTrace

name: taskRefreshUser
doc: Refresh the user account used for tasks

name: tan
doc: Return tangent of angle in radians.

name: ruleTargetsUnder
doc:
Get a grid of all the rule targets which are contained by the given
parent.  For example given a site record, get a list of all rule targets
under that site including spaces, equips, and points with a corresponding
siteRef.  This function is more effcicient than using `navResolve()`
because it only returns records which are known to be rule targets in
the current roster for the local project.

Example:
  // query all sparks under a given site
  read(site).ruleTargetsUnder.ruleSparks(lastMonth)

name: eval
doc:
Evaluate an Axon string expression.  The evaluation happens in a new
scope and does not have access to the current scope of local variables.
Also see `call()` and `toAxonCode()`.

Examples:
eval("2 + 2")
eval("now()")

name: hisFindPeaks
doc:
Filter a history grid so that only numeric samples that represent
a peak are returned.  A peak is defined as a timestamp/value
pair where both the previous and next value is smaller.  Non-numeric
columns are filtered out.  If the source grid has multiple
numeric value columns, then non-peak cells are set to null.
Also see `hisFindTroughs`.

name: unit
doc:
Given a number return its unit string or null.
If the val is null, then return null.

name: acos
doc: Return the arc cosine.

name: xqReadAllList
doc: Read recs by filter - see `skyarcd::XQuery.readAllList`

name: hisMap
doc:
Perform a map transformation on the history value cells.  The
mapping function takes '(val, ts, his)' and returns new vals.
The src must be a history grid.

By default, empty columns will be removed. To keep empty columns you may
do one of the following:
  1. pass the 'hisKeepEmptyCols' option to `hisRead`
  1. add the 'hisKeepEmptyCols' marker tag to the history grid meta
     using `addMeta`

name: ioMove
doc:
Move or rename a file or directory.  Both handles must reference
a local file or directory on the file system.  If the target file
already exists then raise an IOErr.

name: reFindAll
doc:
Find all matches of the regular expression in 's'.
Returns an empty list of there are no matches.

Examples:
reFindAll(r"-?\d+\.?\d*", "foo, 123, bar, 456.78, -9, baz")
>> ["123", "456.78", "-9"]
reFindAll(r"\d+", "foo, bar, baz")
>> [,]

name: matrixSub
doc:
Subtract two matrices and return new matrix.  The parameters may
be any value supported `toMatrix`.  Matrices must have the same dimensions.

name: def
doc:
Lookup a def by its symbol name (Str or Symbol).  If not
found return null or raise UnknownDefErr based on checked flag.
The result is returned as the definition's normalized dict
representation.

name: isHisGrid
doc: Return if an object is a grid that conforms to the [history grid shape]`haystack::Grid.isHisGrid`

name: addRows
doc:
Add an list of rows to the end of a grid.
The newRows may be expressed as list of Dict or a Grid.

Example:
readAll(site).addRows(readAll(equip))

name: ioWriteTrio
doc:
Write dicts to a [Trio]`docHaystack::Trio` file.
The 'val' may be can be any format accepted by `toRecList`.

Following options are supported
- noSort: marker to prevent tags from being sorted by name

name: bitAnd
doc: Bitwise and: 'a & b'

name: foldCol
doc:
Fold the values of the given column into a single value.
The folding function uses the same semantics as `fold`.

Example:
readAll(site).foldCol("area", sum)

name: taskSend
doc:
Asynchronously send a message to the given task for processing.
Return a future to track the asynchronous result.
See `lib-task::doc#messaging`.

name: firstOfMonth
doc:
Get the first day of given date's month.
Also see `lastOfMonth()`.

Example:
2009-10-28.firstOfMonth  >>  2009-10-01

name: hisClip
doc:
Remove the rows with leading/trailing timestamps from the
actual query range.  The query range is defined as inclusive
of 'meta.hisStart' and exclusive of 'meta.hisEnd'.  These
meta tags are derived from the dates range passed to the `hisRead`
function.

Example:
  hisRead(points, dates).hisClip

name: funcs
doc:
Find all the top-levels functions in the current project
which match the given filter. If the filter is omitted then
return all the functions declared in the current project.
The function tags are returned.

Examples:
funcs()              // all functions
funcs(sparkRule)     // match filter

name: dis
doc:
Get display string for dict or the given tag.  If 'name'
is null, then return display text for the entire dict
using `haystack::Etc.dictToDis`.  If 'name' is non-null then format
the tag value using its appropiate 'toLocale' method.
Also see `haystack::Dict.dis`.

name: logRead
doc:
Read log records for given span.  A non-null filter
will be applied to the log records.  Opts:
  - 'limit': max number of recs to return (default 10,000)
NOTE: log reads for today cannot increase the limit past
what is configured in Settings|Log for bufMax.

name: energyNormByArea
doc:
Given a his grid, normalize the values by area in m² or ft².  Each
value column must have a `siteRef` which references a site with
the `area` tag.

name: read
doc:
Read from database the first record which matches [filter]`docHaystack::Filters`.
If no matches found throw UnknownRecErr or null based on checked
flag.  If there are multiple matches it is indeterminate which one is
returned.  See `readAll` for how filter works.

Examples:
read(site)                 // read any site rec
read(site and dis=="HQ")   // read site rec with specific dis tag
read(chiller)              // raise exception if no recs with chiller tag
read(chiller, false)       // return null if no recs with chiller tag

name: exts
doc:
Get the list of exts and their current status.
Result format is subject to change

name: reMatches
doc:
Return if regular expression matches entire region of 's'.
See [AxonUsage]`docHaxall::AxonUsage#regex`.

Examples:
reMatches(r"\d+", "x123y")  >>  false
reMatches(r"\d+", "123")    >>  true

name: toDevices
doc:
Map a set of recs to to a grid of [devices]`device`.  The 'recs'
parameter may be any value accepted by `toRecList()`.  Return empty
grid if no mapping is found.  The following mappings are supported:
- recs with 'device' tag are mapped as themselves
- recs with 'deviceRef' tag are mapped to their parent device
- recs with 'site' are mapped to devices with parent 'siteRef'
- recs with 'space' are mapped to devices with parent 'spaceRef'
- recs with 'equip' are mapped to devices with parent 'equipRef'

Examples:
read(site).toDevices      // return children devices within site
read(space).toDevices     // return children devices within space
read(equip).toDevices     // return children devices within equip
read(point).toDevices     // return point's parent device

name: call
doc:
Reflectively call a function with the given arguments.  The func
may be a Str name or an expression that evaluates to a function.
Args is a positional list for each argument.  Examples:

call("today")
call("replace", ["hi there", "hi", "hello"])
call("parseDate", ["2021-03-15"])
call("parseDate", ["15-Mar-21", "DD-MMM-YY"])
call(parseDate, ["15-Mar-21", "DD-MMM-YY"])
call(parseDate(_, "DD-MMM-YY"), ["15-Mar-21"])

name: pointOverrideCommand
doc:
Issue a point override command based on current user's access
control permissions

name: arcbeamReadByIds
doc:
Read a list of records from remote node by their identifiers.  Return a
grid where each  row of the grid maps to the respective id list (indexes
line up).  If  checked is true and any one of the ids cannot be resolved
then raise UnknownRecErr for first id not resolved.  If checked is false,
then each id not found has a row where every cell is null.

Examples:
  arcbeamReadByIds(@conn, [@abc-123, @xyx-123])

name: parseDateTime
doc:
Parse a Str into a DateTime.  If the string cannot be parsed into a valid
DateTime and checked is false then return null, otherwise throw ParseErr.
See `sys::DateTime.toLocale` for pattern:

YY     Two digit year             07
YYYY   Four digit year            2007
M      One/two digit month        6, 11
MM     Two digit month            06, 11
MMM    Three letter abbr month    Jun, Nov
MMMM   Full month                 June, November
D      One/two digit day          5, 28
DD     Two digit day              05, 28
DDD    Day with suffix            1st, 2nd, 3rd, 24th
WWW    Three letter abbr weekday  Tue
WWWW   Full weekday               Tuesday
V      One/two digit week of year 1,52
VV     Two digit week of year     01,52
VVV    Week of year with suffix   1st,52nd
h      One digit 24 hour (0-23)   3, 22
hh     Two digit 24 hour (0-23)   03, 22
k      One digit 12 hour (1-12)   3, 11
kk     Two digit 12 hour (1-12)   03, 11
m      One digit minutes (0-59)   4, 45
mm     Two digit minutes (0-59)   04, 45
s      One digit seconds (0-59)   4, 45
ss     Two digit seconds (0-59)   04, 45
SS     Optional seconds (only if non-zero)
f*     Fractional secs trailing zeros
F*     Fractional secs no trailing zeros
a      Lower case a/p for am/pm   a, p
aa     Lower case am/pm           am, pm
A      Upper case A/P for am/pm   A, P
AA     Upper case AM/PM           AM, PM
z      Time zone offset           Z, +03:00 (ISO 8601, XML Schema)
zzz    Time zone abbr             EST, EDT
zzzz   Time zone name             New_York
'xyz'  Literal characters
''     Single quote literal

Examples:
parseDateTime("2023-02-07 14:30", "YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm")
parseDateTime("2023-02-07 14:30", "YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm", "Paris")
parseDateTime("7/2/23 2:30pm", "D/M/YY k:mma")
parseDateTime("2023-02-07T14:30:00", "YYYY-MM-DD'T'hh:mm:ss")

name: map
doc:
Map list, dict, or grid by applying the given mapping function.

If mapping a list, the mapping should be a function
that takes '(val)' or '(val, index)'.  It should return
the new value for that index.

If mapping a dict, the mapping should be a function
that takes '(val)' or '(val, name)'.  It should return
the new value for that name.

If mapping a grid, the mapping function takes '(row)' or '(row,index)'
and returns a new dictionary to use for the row.  The resulting
grid shares the original's grid level meta.  Columns
left intact share the old meta-data, new columns have no
meta-data.  If the mapping function returns null, then that row
is removed from the resulting grid (not mapped).

If mapping a range, then the mapping function takes '(integer)', and
returns a list for each mapped integer inte the range.

If mapping a stream, the mapping functions takes '(val)'.
See `docHaxall::Streams#map`.

Examples:
// create list adding ten to each number
[1, 2, 3].map(v => v+10)   >>   [11, 12, 13]

// create new list that turns strings into uppercase
["ape", "bee", "cat"].map(upper)    // ["APE, "BEE", "CAT"]

// create dict adding ten to each value
{a:1, b:2, c:3}.map(v => v+10)   >>   {a:11, b:12, c:13}

// create grid with just dis, area column
readAll(site).map(s => {dis:s->dis, area:s->area})

name: max
doc:
Compare two numbers and return the larger one.  This function
may also be used with `fold` to return the largest number (or
null if no values).  Note number units are **not** checked nor
considered for the comparison.

Examples:
max(7, 4)            >>  7
[7, 2, 4].fold(max)  >>  7

name: ioCharset
doc:
Configure an I/O handle to use the specified charset.  The handle
is any supported [I/O handle]`doc#handles` and the charset is a string
name supported by the JVM installation.  Standard charset names:
- "UTF-8" 8-bit Unicode Transformation Format
- "UTF-16BE": 16 bit Big Endian Unicode Transformation Format
- "UTF-16LE" 16 bit Little Endian Unicode Transformation Format
- "ISO-8859-1": Latin-1 code block
- "US-ASCII": 7-bit ASCII

Examples:
// write text file in UTF-16BE
ioWriteStr(str, ioCharset(`io/foo.txt`, "UTF-16BE"))

// read CSV file in ISO-8859-1
ioCharset(`io/foo.csv`, "ISO-8859-1").ioReadCsv

name: ioDelete
doc:
Delete a file or a directory as mapped by the given I/O handle.
If a directory is specified, then it is recursively deleted.  If the
I/O handle does map to a file system then raise exception.  If the
file does not exist then no action is taken.

name: recDelete
doc:
Record delete action.
This function implicitly operates on all contained recs.
NOTE: this function should only be used for actions.

name: name
doc:
If val is a Col, get the column name.

Example:
// get name of first column
readAll(site).cols.first.name

name: xqTimeout
doc: Set timeout for network calls to remote nodes - see `skyarcd::XQuery.timeout`.

name: connPointsVia
doc:
Select a group of points explicitly via specific connector library
or connector.  This function is required when points are associated
with multiple connectors.  Pass a connector library name to select
for a protocol.  Or pass a connector id/rec to select via a specific
connector.  Also see `docHaxall::Conns#multipleConns`.

Examples:
// explicitly sync via the SQL connector library
readAll(sqlConnRef).connPointsVia("sql").connSyncHis

// get debug details for specific connector
connPointsVia(pt, pt->bacnetConnRef).connDetails

name: recEnable
doc:
Record enable action.
NOTE: this function should only be used for actions.

name: eachWhile
doc:
Iterate the items of a collection until the given function returns
non-null.  Once non-null is returned, then break the iteration and
return the resulting object.  Return null if the function returns null
for every item.
- Grid: iterate the rows as (row, index)
- List: iterate the items as (val, index)
- Dict: iterate the name/value pairs (val, name)
- Str: iterate the characters as numbers (char, index)
- Range: iterate the integer range (integer)
- Stream: iterate items as (val); see `docHaxall::Streams#eachWhile`

name: hisPeriodMatrix
doc:
Get a grid which defines the "matrix" of unique combinations of all the
periods merged together. The 'periods' parameter may be:
  - Grid: a single grid where the first column is a "ts" column,
  and all remaining columns are named anything and contain period values.
  - Grid?[]: a list of grids where each grid has the format described above,

All input grids must be [normalized]`hisPeriodNormalize()`

The result is returned as a grid with these columns:
  - 'ts': starting timestamp of each unique period
  - 'dur': duration in hours of each unique period
  - 'numTrue': number of input periods "on" during period
  - 'v{i}': true of false indicating if input was "on" during that period

Example, lets assume we have three unit runtime periods a, b, and c:
  // look for periods where all units are all off
  hisPeriodMatrix([a, b, c]).findAll r => r->numTrue == 0

  // look for periods where all units are all on
  hisPeriodMatrix([a, b, c]).findAll r => r->numTrue == 3

  // look for periods where a and b are on, but c is off
  hisPeriodMatrix([a, b, c]).findAll r => r->v0 and r->v1 and not r->v2

name: hisPeriodNormalize
doc:
Given a grid of historical periods, merge any overlapping periods to result
in a "normalized" [his period grid]`ext-his::doc#hisPeriodGrid`.

Examples:
  // input periods
  02:00 1hr
  02:30 3hr

  hisPeriodNormalize(src)
  02:00 3.5hr

name: insertAll
doc: Insert a list of items at the given index and return a new list.

name: arcRemovePart
doc:
Remove an existing part on a record. Orig must be an arc record.  The origPart
must be a dict with the current part which you can get from the `arcRead()` grid.
Return new arc document as grid.  This function requires current user to
have "editPart" permission. See `ext-arc::doc#axon`.

name: futureIsComplete
doc:
Return if a future has completed or is still pending a result.
A future is completed by any of the following conditions:
- the task processes the message and returns a result
- the task processes the message and raises an exception
- the future is cancelled
See `lib-task::doc#futures`.

name: navRef
doc:
Return a navigation Ref for given target name and record.
The target should be a string name for the tree leafs such such
"site", "equip", or "point".  The record is an Ref id or Dict
for the node to represent.  Raise exception if the record does
not match any configured level of the target path.  Pass the
string "all" to select the root of the navigation tree.
Also see `docFresco::Nav#navSelAny`.

Examples:
  navRef("point", pt)      // select one point
  navRef("point", equip)   // select all points contained by given equip
  navRef("equip", site)    // select all equips contained by given site

name: ioReadJsonGrid
doc:
Read a JSON file formatted as a standardized Haystack grid
into memory. See `ioReadJson` to read arbitrary JSON structured data.

name: uriIsDir
doc: Return if the URI path ends in a slash.

name: markdownToBlocks
doc: Axon function

name: reFind
doc:
Find the first match of regular expression in 's'
or return null if no matches.
See [AxonUsage]`docHaxall::AxonUsage#regex`.

Examples:
reFind(r"\d+", "x123y")  >>  "123"
reFind(r"\d+", "xyz")    >>  null

name: targetsToRules
doc:
Get all the rule records which are registered on given list
of targets.  The target may be any value accepted by `toRecList`.

name: names
doc: Get the list of names used by a given dict

name: ruleSparks
ruleRosterRefresh
doc: Force [roster refresh]`ext-rule::doc#roster`

name: isUri
doc: Return if an object is a Uri type

name: specType
doc:
Data type of the spec.  Returns the spec itself if given a top-level type.

Examples:
specType(Str)                      >>  sys:Str
spec("ph::Equip.equip").specType   >>  sys::Marker

name: refDis
doc: Given a ref return `haystack::Ref.dis`

name: markdownToHtml
doc: Axon function

name: startOfWeek
doc:
Return current locale's start of weekday.  Weekday is
returned as integer from 0 (Sunday) to 6 (Saturday).

name: hisPeriodIntersection
doc:
Find the intersection of two or more historical periods. The 'periods'
parameter may be:
  - Grid: a single grid where the first column is a "ts" column,
  and all remaining columns are named anything and contain period values.
  - Grid?[]: a list of grids where each grid has the format described above

All input grids must be [normalized]`hisPeriodNormalize()`

The resulting grid has "ts" and "v0" column, where "v0" is duration in hours.

Example:
  // find periods when both a and b are on
  hisPeriodIntersection([a, b])

name: chart
doc: Set the grid visualization view to chart.

name: isBool
doc: Return if an object is a boolean type

name: hisInterpolate
doc:
Interpolate all the null value cells for each timestamp.
See [Interpolation]`ext-hisKit::doc#interpolation` for details.

name: specSlotsOwn
bitOr
doc: Bitwise or: 'a | b'

name: toHex
doc: Convert a number to a hexadecimal string.

name: discreteBoolPeriods
doc:
Fold timestamp/bool value pairs into an encoded Str of discrete minutely
intervals. This rollup is similar to `discretePeriods` with the same
Base64 encoding. There are two significant differences:
  1. This rollup supports NA data
  2. Instead of encoding offset/duration pairs, the encoding is
     offset/ordinal pairs. The ordinal values map as '{0: false, 1: true, 2: NA}'

Also see `parseDiscreteBoolPeriods` and `haystack::Etc.discretePeriods`.

name: toRecId
doc:
Coerce a value to a Ref identifier:
- Ref returns itself
- Row or Dict, return 'id' tag
- Grid return first row id

name: log
doc:
Submit a log record to the logging subsystem:
  - level: must be "err", "warn", "info", "debug" (same as `sys::LogLevel`).
  - tags: Dict for `skyarcd::LogMod.create` or Str which is assumed to be '{name:<str>}'
  - msg: short single line summary of message to log
  - err: exception from catch block that includes errTrace tag

Best practice is to pass a SysMod/Ext name for the 'tags' parameter,
in which case the SysMod/Ext log is used.  This allows you to manage
the logging level via the Debug|Log view.  If you pass an unknown library
name or a Dict for 'tags' then a temporary log instance is created,
which cannot have its logging level configured.

Also see `logErr()`, `logWarn()`, `logInfo()`, and `logDebug()` .

Examples:
  // use the host system module logger and current log level
  log("info", "host", "something happened")

  // convenience for previous
  logInfo("host", "something happened")

  // use the point extension logger and current log level
  logErr("point", "something happened")

  // log to my custom log - log level not configurable
  logWarn("myOwnLog", "something happened")

  // log to custom log name with additional tags
  logWarn({name:"myOwnLog", user:"alice"}, "something happened")

  /// example of logging with a stack trace
  try throw "bad!" catch (e) logErr("task", "Failed", e)

name: specLibs
doc:
List Xeto libraries as a list of their [dict]`xeto::Lib` representation.
Is scope is null then return all installed libs (libs not yet loaded
will not have their metadata).  Otherwise scope must be a filter
expression used to filter the dict representation.

Examples:
specLibs()             // all installed libs
specLibs(loaded)       // only libs loaded into memory

name: reorderCols
doc:
Return a new grid with the columns reordered.  The given list
of names represents the new order and must contain the same current
column names.  Any columns not specified are removed.  Also
see `colNames`, `moveTo`, and `docHaxall::Streams#reorderCols`.

Example:
// move name to first col, and foo to last col
cols: grid.colNames.moveTo("name", 0).moveTo("foo", -1)
return grid.reorderCols(cols)

name: day
doc: Get day of month as integer between 1 to 31 from date or datetime.

name: ruleSparkHis
doc:
Turn a grid of sparks as returned by `ruleSparks` into a history grid
of the spark periods.  Each unique combination of 'targetRef' and
'ruleRef' becomes the value columns.

name: context
doc:
Get the current context as a Dict with the following tags:
- 'username' for current user
- 'userRef' id for current user
- 'locale' current locale

SkySpark tags:
- 'projName' if evaluating in context of a project
- 'nodeId' local cluster node id
- 'ruleRef' if evaluating in context of a rule engine
- 'ruleTuning' if evaluating in context of rule engine

name: task
doc: Lookup a task by id which is any value supported by `toRecId()`.

name: parseFloat
doc:
Parse a Str into a Float.  Representations for infinity and
not-a-number are "-INF", "INF", "NaN".  If invalid format
and checked is false return null, otherwise throw ParseErr.
This string value *cannot* include a unit (see parseNumber).

Examples:
parseFloat("123.456").format("0.000")
parseFloat("NaN")
parseFloat("INF")

name: geoUsStateCode
doc:
Normalize a US state name into its standard two letter postal code.
If the state is not recognized, then return null or throw exception
based on checked flag.

name: lastWeek
doc: DateSpan for week previous to this week 'sun..sat' (uses locale start of week)

name: xqFindAll
doc: Filter the previous step using an Axon expr - see `skyarcd::XQuery.findAll`

name: thisYear
doc: DateSpan for this year 'Jan-1..Dec-31'

name: connSyncCur
doc:
Perform a remote sync of current values for the given points.
The 'points' parameter may be anything acceptable by `toRecIdList()`.
Return a list of futures for each unique connector.  The result
of each future is unspecified.  Also see `docHaxall::Conns#cur`.

Examples:
readAll(bacnetCur).connSyncCur

name: localeUse
doc:
Evaluate an expression within a specific locale.  This enables
formatting and parsing of localized text using a locale other than
the default for the current context.

Examples:
// format Date in German
localeUse("de", today().format)

// parse Date in German
localeUse("de", parseDate("01 Mär 2021", "DD MMM YYYY"))

name: random
doc:
Return random integer within given inclusive range.
If range is null, then full range of representative
integers is assumed.

Examples:
random()       // random num with no range
random(0..100) // random num between 0 and 100

name: rulesToTargets
doc:
Get all the target records the list of rules is registered on by
the `ruleOn` tag.  The rule may be any value accepted by `toRecList`.

name: validateTagName
doc: Raise an exception if string is not a validate tag name

name: energyBaselinePrevMonth
doc:
Generate a baseline his grid based on last month's data.
Data from exactly 28days ago is used to ensure alignment by weekday.

name: watchOpen
doc:
Open a new watch on a grid of records.  The 'dis' parameter
is used for the watch's debug display string.  Update and return
the grid with a meta 'watchId' tag.  Also see `hx::HxWatchService.open`
and `docHaxall::Watches#axon`.

Example:
readAll(myPoints).watchOpen("MyApp|Points")

name: connTrace
doc:
Read a connector trace log as a grid.  If tracing is not enabled
for the given connector, then an empty grid is returned.  The 'conn'
parameter may be anything acceptable by `toRecId()`.

Examples:
read(conn).connTrace
connTrace(connId)

name: ioWriteJsonLd
doc:
Write an Axon data structure to RDF [JSON-LD]`docHaystack::Rdf` format.
The 'val' must be an Axon type that can be converted to a Grid.

name: hisDailyProfile
doc:
Perform a rollup by time slice to produce a single 24-hour
profile of the data. The input must be a history grid with
a 'ts' DateTime column and timestamps spread across a uniform
interval (typically result of `hisRollup`).  The result
replaces 'ts' with a 'time' column for each unique time-slice.

For example, let's consider we wish to generate an average
daily profile of our energy use in Jan of 2010.  Assuming
have 15-min demand data we would start with this rollup:

  hisRead(pt, 2010-01).hisRollup(max, 1hr)

This produces a grid with one row for each hour of the month.
The values of the grid are the peak demand within each hour
window (using the `max` function).  Now let's say we wish
to average the entire month to get average peak for each
one hour time slice:

  hisRead(pt, 2010-01).hisRollup(max, 1hr).hisDailyProfile(avg)

The resulting grid will have 24 rows with the average of each
time-slice:

  time    val
  -----   ----
  00:00   avg(01-Jan 00:00, 02-Jan 00:00, ... 31-Jan 00:00)
  01:00   avg(01-Jan 01:00, 02-Jan 01:00, ... 31-Jan 01:00)
  ....
  23:00   avg(01-Jan 23:00, 02-Jan 23:00, ... 31-Jan 23:00)

name: isEmpty
doc: Return if a collection is empty: str, list, dict, or grid

name: meta
doc:
Get the meta-data from a grid or col as a dict.

Examples:
read(temp).hisRead(today).meta             // grid meta
read(temp).hisRead(today).col("ts").meta   // column meta

name: taskRun
doc:
Run the given expression asynchronously in an ephemeral task.
Return a future to track the asynchronous result.  Note the
expr passed cannot use any variables from the current scope.
See `lib-task::doc#ephemeralTasks`.

name: hour
doc: Get hour of day as integer between 0 to 23 from time or datetime

name: find
doc:
Find the first matching item in a list or grid by
applying the given filter function.  If no match
is found return null.

If working with a list, the filter should be a function
that takes '(val)' or '(val, index)'.  It should return
true to match and return the item.

If working with a dict, the filter should be a function
that takes '(val)' or '(val, name)'.  It should return
true to match and return the item.

If working with a grid, the filter function takes '(row)'
or '(row, index)' and returns true to match and return the row.

If working with a stream, the filter takes '(val)' and returns
true to match.  See `docHaxall::Streams#find`.

Examples:
// find first string longer than 3 chars
["ape", "bat", "charlie", "dingo"].find(x => x.size > 3)

// find first odd number
[10, 4, 3, 7].find(isOdd)

name: ruleErrs
doc:
Return grid of all the rule+targets which have errors.  The rules
parameter may be any set of recs accepted by `toRecList` or null
to query all rules.  This function does **not** automatically refresh
the roster.  The grid contains the following columns:
  - ruleRef: id of the rule rec
  - targetRef: id of the target or null if entire rule is in fault
  - status: type of error
  - errTrace: last exception (if rule has been run since reboot)

name: readAllTagVals
doc:
Return the range of all the values mapped to a given
tag name used by all the records matching the given filter.
This method is capped to 200 results.  The results are
returned as a grid with a single 'val' column.
Also see `readAllTagNames`.

Examples:
// read grid of all unique point unit tags
readAllTagVals(point, "unit")

name: sort
doc:
Sort a list or grid.

If sorting a list, the sorter should be a function
that takes two list items and returns -1, 0, or 1 (typicaly
done with the '<=>' operator.  If no sorter is passed, then
the list is sorted by its natural ordering.

If sorting a grid, the sorter can be a column name
or a function.  If a function, it should take two rows
and return -1, 0, or 1.

Examples:
// sort string list
["bear", "cat", "apple"].sort

// sort string list by string size
["bear", "cat", "apple"].sort((a,b) => a.size <=> b.size)

// sort sites by area
readAll(site).sort((a, b) => a->area <=> b->area)

name: curFunc
doc: Get the current top-level function's tags.

name: end
doc: End value of a DateSpan, Span, or a range.

name: fits
doc:
Return if the given instance fits the spec via structural typing.
Use `specFits()` to check structural typing between two types.
Also see `is()` and `specIs()` to check via nominal typing.  Use
`fitsExplain()` to explain why fits returns true or false.

If the val is a Dict, then the default behavior is to only check
the dict's tags against the given spec.  In this mode all spec query
slots are ignored.  Pass the '{graph}' option to also check
queries to validate the graph of entities.  For example, the graph
option can be used with equip specs to validate required points.


Options:
- 'graph': marker to also check graph of references such as required points
- 'ignoreRefs': marker to not validate if refs exist or match target spec

Examples:
fits("foo", Str)               >>  true
fits(123, Str)                 >>  false
fits(equipRec, Equip)          >>  true
fits(equipRec, Site)           >>  false
fits(vav, MyVavSpec)           >> validate tags only
fits(vav, MyVavSpec, {graph})  >> validate tags and required points

name: vizAutoRange
doc:
Return the automatic axis range of a min, max number.
The range is returned as a dict of '{min, max, interval}'.

Examples:
  vizAutoRange(22, 99)

name: toTagName
doc:
Given arbitrary string, convert to a safe tag name -
see `haystack::Etc.toTagName`

name: query
doc:
Evaluate a relationship query and return record dict.  If no matches
found throw UnknownRecErr or return null based on checked flag.
If there are multiple matches it is indeterminate which one is
returned.  Subject must be a record id or dict in the database.  Spec
must be a Spec typed as a 'sys::Query'.  Also see `queryAll`.

Example:
read(point).query(spec("ph::Point.equips"))

name: readAllTagNames
doc:
Return the intersection of all tag names used by all the records
matching the given filter.  The results are returned as a grid
with following columns:
- 'name': string name of the tag
- 'kind': all the different value kinds separated by "|"
- 'count': total number of recs with the tag
Also see `readAllTagVals` and `gridColKinds`.

Examples:
// read statistics on all tags used by equip recs
readAllTagNames(equip)

name: userIsProjAccessible
doc:
Return if the given user has access to the project as configured
by `projAccessFilter`.  The user must be a Dict and the projName
must be a Str name.  Also see `skyarc::User.isProjAccessible`.
Requires su permission.

name: navProjNames
doc:
Return a list of string project names which are currently
navigable based on current [uiMeta]`docFresco::Nav#uiMeta` flag

name: extAdd
doc:
Enable the given extension name or list of names.
All dependencies must be already enabled.

Examples:
   extAdd("bacnet")
   extAdd(["bacnet", "opc"])

name: toAxonCode
doc:
Convert a scalar, list, or dict value to its Axon code representation.
Examples:

toAxonCode(123)        =>   "123"
toAxonCode([1, 2, 3])  =>   "[1, 2, 3]"
toAxonCode({x:123})    =>   "{x:123}"

name: nan
doc: Return the Number representation of not-a-number

name: toDateSpan
doc:
Convert the following objects into a `haystack::DateSpan`:
- 'Func': function which evaluates to date range
- 'DateSpan': return itself
- 'Date': one day range
- 'Span': return `haystack::Span.toDateSpan`
- 'Str': evaluates to `haystack::DateSpan.fromStr`
- 'Date..Date': starting and ending date (inclusive)
- 'Date..Number': starting date and num of days (day unit required)
- 'DateTime..DateTime': use starting/ending dates; if end is midnight,
then use previous date
- 'Number': convert as year
- null: use projMeta dateSpanDefault or default to today (deprecated)

Examples:
toDateSpan(2010-07-01..2010-07-03)  >>  01-Jul-2010..03-Jul-2010
toDateSpan(2010-07-01..60day)       >>  01-Jul-2010..29-Aug-2010
toDateSpan(2010-07)                 >>  01-Jul-2010..31-Jul-2010
toDateSpan(2010)                    >>  01-Jan-2010..31-Dec-2010
toDateSpan(pastWeek) // on 9 Aug    >>  02-Aug-2010..09-Aug-2010

name: haystackLearn
doc: Deprecated - use `connLearn()`

name: unique
doc:
Return the unique items in a collection.  If val is a List
then return `sys::List.unique`.  If val is a Grid then
return `haystack::Grid.unique` where key must be a column
name or list of column names.

Examples:
[1, 1, 2, 2].unique                 >> [1, 2]
grid.unique("geoState")             >> unique states
grid.unique(["geoCity", geoState"]) >> city,state combos

name: discretePeriods
doc:
Fold timestamp/bool value pairs into an encoded Str of discrete minutely
intervals.  The string encoding is based on 12-bit minutely
start/duration pairs.  Each 12-bit minute offset/duration is encoded
using two 6-bit ASCII characters encoded by the Base64 table.
Also see `parseDiscretePeriods` and `haystack::Etc.discretePeriods`.

Here is the Fantom code which decodes a string to iterate a
period where 'time' is offset in minutes from base timestamp
and 'dur' is duration of period in minutes (assuming a minutely
interval).

    static Void decodePeriod(Str str, |Int time, Int dur| f)
    {
      for (i := 0; i<str.size; i += 4)
      {
        time := fromBase64[str[i+0]].shiftl(6) + fromBase64[str[i+1]]
        dur  := fromBase64[str[i+2]].shiftl(6) + fromBase64[str[i+3]]
        f(time, dur)
      }
    }

    // base64 lookup tables
    static const Str toBase64 :=
      "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZabcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz0123456789+/"
    static const Int[] fromBase64
    static
    {
      list := Int[,].fill(0, 128)
      toBase64.each |ch, i| { list[ch] = i }
      fromBase64 = list
    }

Note: the encoding size of 12-bits limits representations to offsets
of 4095 minutes or roughly 2.8 days.  Using using rollup intervals
over 1day cannot be represented using this encoding and will be silently
truncated past 2.8 days.

name: upper
doc:
Convert a char number or str to ASCII upper case.
Also see `lower()` and `capitalize()`.

Examples:
upper("cat")      >> "CAT"
upper("Cat")      >> "CAT"
upper(97).toChar  >> "A"

name: xobjExts
doc:
Return all the XObj extensions installed across the cluster
   - node: node id
   - proj: name of the project
   - ext: name of the extension

name: pods
doc: Return list of installed Fantom pods

name: ruleTest
doc:
Perform test run of rule.  The rule may be a rule ready function
or a rule record itself.  If the target is omitted, then a target is
auto-selected by 'ruleOn'.  If date is omitted and the rule is
is spark or KPI then yesterday will be used.

The tuning parameter is an optional dict of tuning name/value pairs
which is used to bind to to any cells with a 'bindTuning' tags.
If the given rule already has rule tuning, then the tuning
parameter will override tags from the current configuration.

NOTE: this function's parameters and output is subject to change.

Examples:
  // rule ready function - pass function by name
  ruleTest(ahuCoolAndHeat)

  // rule ready function with explicit target
  ruleTest(ahuCoolAndHeat, @2b80f96a-ee1d1270)

  // rule ready function with explicit target and date
  ruleTest(ahuCoolAndHeat, @p:demo:r:2b80f96a-ee1d1270, 2023-03-01)

  // rule record
  read(rule and dis=="AHU Fan Failure").ruleTest

name: readAll
doc:
Reall all records from the database which match the [filter]`docHaystack::Filters`.
The filter must an expression which matches the filter structure.
String values may parsed into a filter using `parseFilter` function.

Options:
- 'limit': max number of recs to return
- 'sort': sort by display name

Examples:
readAll(site)                      // read all site recs
readAll(equip and siteRef==@xyz)   // read all equip in a given site
readAll(equip, {limit:10})         // read up to ten equips
readAll(equip, {sort})             // read all equip sorted by dis

name: stream
doc:
Create new stream from given collection:
- Grid: stream the rows
- List: stream the items
- Range: stream inclusive range of integers
See `docHaxall::Streams#stream`.

name: toRecList
doc:
Coerce a value to a list of record Dicts:
- null return empty list
- Ref or Ref[] (will make a call to read database)
- Row or Row[] returns itself
- Dict or Dict[] returns itself
- Grid is mapped to list of rows

name: readByIds
doc:
